1→# Feature Specification: Agent Messaging — Direct Communication & Chat History
     2→
     3→**Feature Branch**: `006-agent-messaging`
     4→**Created**: 2026-02-22
     5→**Status**: Draft
     6→**Authors**: Chairman + Claude
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## Context
    11→
    12→### The Problem
    13→
    14→Today, ClawCorp agents can only communicate through **formal MCP tools**:
    15→
    16→| Tool | Direction | Use Case |
    17→|---|---|---|
    18→| `escalate` | Up (to supervisor) | Blocking question, waits for answer |
    19→| `report` | Up (to supervisor) | Task completion notification |
    20→| `delegate` | Down (to subordinate) | Assigns work, spawns sub-mission |
    21→| `cross_team_delegate` | Lateral (director→director) | Cross-department task handoff |
    22→
    23→This is **command-and-control**, not **communication**. Every interaction is either "I'm blocked, help" or "I'm done, here's what I did" or "Go do this."
    24→
    25→What's missing is the **everyday workplace conversation** — the kind that happens in Slack, Teams, or walking over to someone's desk:
    26→
    27→- "Hey, I noticed the auth module uses bcrypt — was there a reason you didn't pick argon2?"
    28→- "FYI: I refactored the database schema, your migration scripts might need updating"
    29→- "Quick question — what format does your API return dates in?"
    30→- "Can you review this approach before I implement it?"
    31→
    32→These aren't escalations (not blocked), reports (not done), or delegations (not assigning work). They're **peer conversations** — the glue of any functioning organization.
    33→
    34→### The Vision
    35→
    36→Replace the rigid MCP-tool-only communication with a **messaging system**:
    37→
    38→1. **Any agent can message any agent** — not just up/down the chain
    39→2. **Messages are persistent** — stored in a shared channel-like structure, browsable by Chairman
    40→3. **No blocking** — unlike `escalate` which polls for 10 minutes, messages are fire-and-forget. The recipient reads them on their next run.
    41→4. **Thread support** — reply to a specific message, creating a conversation thread
    42→5. **Chairman can see everything** — all inter-agent communication is transparent, browsable, searchable
    43→6. **Future-proof for Slack integration** — the data model mirrors Slack's channel/thread structure so we can bridge to Slack later
    44→
    45→### Analogy
    46→
    47→Think of it like an internal company Slack:
    48→
    49→```
    50→#general            → org-wide announcements
    51→#engineering        → engineering team channel
    52→#research-lab       → research team channel
    53→@senior-engineer    → direct messages to a specific agent
    54→```
    55→
    56→Agents post messages. Other agents read them on their next run. Chairman watches everything in real time from the Control Panel.
    57→
    58→---
    59→
    60→## Architecture
    61→
    62→### Channels
    63→
    64→A **channel** is a named conversation stream. Three types:
    65→
    66→| Type | Naming | Who can read/write | Example |
    67→|---|---|---|---|
    68→| `org` | `#general` | All agents + Chairman | Org-wide announcements |
    69→| `team` | `#engineering`, `#research-lab` | Team members + Chairman | Team discussions |
    70→| `direct` | `@senior-engineer:qa-engineer` | The two agents + Chairman | 1:1 conversations |
    71→
    72→Channels are created automatically:
    73→- `#general` exists by default
    74→- `#engineering`, `#research-lab`, `#product` created when teams exist
    75→- Direct channels created on first message between two agents
    76→
    77→### Messages
    78→
    79→```json
    80→{
    81→  "id": "CM-1740200000000-1",
    82→  "channel": "#engineering",
    83→  "from": "senior-engineer",
    84→  "text": "Heads up: I refactored the auth module to use JWT. If your code imports from auth/session.ts, it's now auth/jwt.ts",
    85→  "replyTo": null,
    86→  "mentions": ["qa-engineer", "intern"],
    87→  "ts": "2026-02-22T10:15:00Z"
    88→}
    89→```
    90→
    91→Replies form threads:
    92→```json
    93→{
    94→  "id": "CM-1740200000001-2",
    95→  "channel": "#engineering",
    96→  "from": "qa-engineer",
    97→  "text": "Got it — I'll update my test imports. Do the function signatures stay the same?",
    98→  "replyTo": "CM-1740200000000-1",
    99→  "mentions": ["senior-engineer"],
   100→  "ts": "2026-02-22T10:15:30Z"
   101→}
   102→```
   103→
   104→### Storage
   105→
   106→```
   107→channels/
   108→├── _channels.json          ← channel registry
   109→├── general/
   110→│   └── messages.jsonl      ← append-only log (one JSON per line)
   111→├── engineering/
   112→│   └── messages.jsonl
   113→├── research-lab/
   114→│   └── messages.jsonl
   115→└── dm-senior-engineer-qa-engineer/
   116→    └── messages.jsonl
   117→```
   118→
   119→Using **JSONL** (one JSON object per line) for channels because:
   120→- Append-only (no rewrite on every message)
   121→- Efficiently tail-able for recent messages
   122→- Each line is a self-contained message
   123→
   124→### Agent Interface — `send_message` MCP Tool
   125→
   126→Agents get a new, simple MCP tool:
   127→
   128→```
   129→send_message(
   130→  to: string,        // channel "#engineering" or agent "@qa-engineer"
   131→  text: string,       // message content (markdown ok)
   132→  reply_to?: string   // optional message ID to thread on
   133→) → { messageId: string }
   134→```
   135→
   136→This replaces **nothing** — escalate/report/delegate stay as they are for their specific workflows. `send_message` is for everything else.
   137→
   138→### Agent Interface — `read_messages` MCP Tool
   139→
   140→```
   141→read_messages(
   142→  channel?: string,    // "#engineering" or "@architect" — omit for all unread
   143→  limit?: number       // default 20
   144→) → { messages: Message[] }
   145→```
   146→
   147→Returns recent messages in a channel, or all unread messages across channels mentioning this agent.
   148→
   149→### How Agents Discover Messages
   150→
   151→On each agent run, the system prompt is augmented with:
   152→
   153→```
   154→You have {N} unread messages. Use read_messages() to check them before starting work.
   155→```
   156→
   157→This is a **hint, not a mandate**. Agents can ignore it if their task is urgent. But it encourages agents to stay aware of organizational context.
   158→
   159→---
   160→
   161→## User Scenarios & Testing
   162→
   163→### User Story 1 — Agent sends a message to a peer (Priority: P1)
   164→
   165→As an agent, I want to send a message to another agent — not as an escalation or delegation, but as a simple FYI, question, or coordination note.
   166→
   167→**Independent Test**: senior-engineer calls `send_message(to: "@qa-engineer", text: "I'm about to refactor the test helpers, hold off on writing new tests")`. Message appears in the direct channel and in the Control Panel.
   168→
   169→**Acceptance Scenarios**:
   170→
   171→1. **Given** senior-engineer sends a message to @qa-engineer, **When** qa-engineer runs next, **Then** qa-engineer can call `read_messages()` and see the message
   172→2. **Given** a message is sent, **When** Chairman views the inbox, **Then** the message appears in the chat log with sender, recipient, timestamp
   173→3. **Given** a message has `mentions: ["intern"]`, **When** intern runs, **Then** intern sees the message in their unread list
   174→
   175→---
   176→
   177→### User Story 2 — Team channel discussions (Priority: P1)
   178→
   179→As an agent, I want to post a message to my team's channel so all teammates are informed.
   180→
   181→**Independent Test**: architect posts to `#engineering`: "Architecture review: I'm moving to a monorepo structure. See /docs/adr-005.md". All engineering team members can read it.
   182→
   183→**Acceptance Scenarios**:
   184→
   185→1. **Given** architect posts to #engineering, **When** any engineering agent calls `read_messages(channel: "#engineering")`, **Then** they see the message
   186→2. **Given** a message is posted to #engineering, **When** Chairman views the channel, **Then** the message is visible with full context
   187→3. **Given** research-assistant tries to post to #engineering, **When** they're not in the Engineering team, **Then** they still can post (channels are open by default — trust the agents)
   188→
   189→---
   190→
   191→### User Story 3 — Chairman browses all conversations (Priority: P1)
   192→
   193→As Chairman, I want to browse all inter-agent communication — see what agents are discussing, who's talking to whom, what decisions are being made informally.
   194→
   195→**Independent Test**: Open a "Chat" tab in Control Panel. See a list of channels. Click a channel to see its messages as a chat timeline. Click a thread to see the replies.
   196→
   197→**Acceptance Scenarios**:
   198→
   199→1. **Given** 3 channels have messages, **When** I open the Chat tab, **Then** I see all channels listed with message counts and last activity time
   200→2. **Given** I click #engineering, **When** the channel opens, **Then** I see messages in chronological order, each showing: sender avatar, name, timestamp, text
   201→3. **Given** a message has replies (thread), **When** I click "N replies", **Then** the thread expands inline showing all replies
   202→
   203→---
   204→
   205→### User Story 4 — Message threads for focused discussions (Priority: P2)
   206→
   207→As an agent, I want to reply to a specific message (creating a thread) to keep discussions organized.
   208→
   209→**Acceptance Scenarios**:
   210→
   211→1. **Given** qa-engineer reads a message from senior-engineer, **When** qa-engineer calls `send_message(to: "#engineering", text: "...", reply_to: "CM-xxx")`, **Then** the reply is linked to the original message as a thread
   212→2. **Given** a message has 3 replies, **When** Chairman views the channel, **Then** the original message shows "3 replies" and the thread is collapsible
   213→
   214→---
   215→
   216→### User Story 5 — Chairman sends messages to agents (Priority: P2)
   217→
   218→As Chairman, I want to send a message to an agent or a channel — to give context, instructions, or feedback outside of formal missions.
   219→
   220→**Acceptance Scenarios**:
   221→
   222→1. **Given** I type a message in #general from the UI, **When** I send it, **Then** it appears in the channel as from "chairman"
   223→2. **Given** I DM @senior-engineer, **When** senior-engineer runs next, **Then** the message appears in their unread
   224→
   225→---
   226→
   227→### User Story 6 — Slack bridge (Priority: P3, future)
   228→
   229→As Chairman, I want agent messages to be mirrored to a real Slack workspace — so I can read agent conversations on my phone and reply from Slack.
   230→
   231→**Acceptance Scenarios**:
   232→
   233→1. **Given** Slack integration is configured, **When** an agent posts to #engineering, **Then** the message appears in the linked Slack channel
   234→2. **Given** I reply in Slack, **When** the agent runs next, **Then** it sees my Slack reply as a regular message
   235→
   236→---
   237→
   238→### Edge Cases
   239→
   240→- What if an agent sends a message during a mission and the mission fails? → Message is still stored. Messages are independent of mission lifecycle.
   241→- What if two agents message each other simultaneously? → No conflict — JSONL is append-only, timestamps resolve ordering.
   242→- Should agents be required to read messages before working? → No. The prompt hint is advisory. Some tasks are urgent and shouldn't block on catching up.
   243→- Can agents send messages when not running a mission? → No. Agents only exist when spawned by AgentRunner. Messages are sent during active runs.
   244→- Message size limit? → 4000 characters per message (roughly 1 Slack message). Longer content should be written to a file and linked.
   245→- Rate limiting? → No explicit limit. If an agent spams, it wastes its own tokens. The Chairman can see it in logs.
   246→- Do channels need to be created explicitly? → Team channels auto-created from teams. DM channels auto-created on first message. `#general` always exists.
   247→
   248→---
   249→
   250→## Requirements
   251→
   252→### Data Model
   253→
   254→**Channel registry** (`channels/_channels.json`):
   255→```json
   256→{
   257→  "channels": [
   258→    { "id": "general", "name": "#general", "type": "org", "created_at": "..." },
   259→    { "id": "engineering", "name": "#engineering", "type": "team", "teamId": "engineering", "created_at": "..." },
   260→    { "id": "dm-senior-engineer-qa-engineer", "name": "@senior-engineer ↔ @qa-engineer", "type": "direct", "participants": ["senior-engineer", "qa-engineer"], "created_at": "..." }
   261→  ]
   262→}
   263→```
   264→
   265→**Message** (one line in `channels/{channelId}/messages.jsonl`):
   266→```json
   267→{"id":"CM-1740200000000-1","channel":"engineering","from":"architect","text":"...","replyTo":null,"mentions":[],"ts":"2026-02-22T10:15:00Z"}
   268→```
   269→
   270→### Server Changes
   271→
   272→**`server/src/routes/channels.ts`** (new) — Channel & message API:
   273→- `GET /api/channels` → list all channels with metadata (message count, last activity)
   274→- `GET /api/channels/:id/messages?limit=50&before=` → paginated messages for a channel
   275→- `POST /api/channels/:id/messages` → post a message (from Chairman UI)
   276→- `POST /api/channels/send` → agent sends a message (called by MCP tool)
   277→- `GET /api/channels/unread/:agentId` → get unread messages mentioning an agent
   278→
   279→**`server/src/mcp/server.ts`** (edit) — add agent tools:
   280→- `send_message` tool — posts to a channel or DM
   281→- `read_messages` tool — reads recent or unread messages
   282→
   283→**`server/src/lib/AgentRunner.ts`** (edit) — unread message hint:
   284→- Before spawning agent, count unread messages for this agent
   285→- If > 0, append to system prompt: `"You have {N} unread messages. Use read_messages() to check them."`
   286→
   287→### Client Changes
   288→
   289→**`client/src/App.tsx`** (edit) — add Chat tab:
   290→- New `chat` tab alongside tasks, inbox, agents
   291→- Left sidebar: channel list with unread indicators
   292→- Main area: message timeline for selected channel
   293→- Thread expansion (click "N replies" to expand)
   294→- Message input at bottom for Chairman to send messages
   295→- WebSocket handlers for `chat:message` events
   296→
   297→### WebSocket Events
   298→
   299→- `chat:message` → `{ channel, message }` — real-time message delivery
   300→- `chat:channel_created` → `{ channel }` — new channel appeared
   301→
   302→### New Files
   303→
   304→| File | Purpose |
   305→|---|---|
   306→| `server/src/routes/channels.ts` | Channel & message REST API |
   307→| `channels/_channels.json` | Channel registry (auto-created) |
   308→| `channels/{id}/messages.jsonl` | Append-only message log per channel |
   309→
   310→### .gitignore
   311→
   312→Add `channels/` to .gitignore (runtime data).
   313→
   314→---
   315→
   316→## Implementation Strategy
   317→
   318→### Phase 1 — Server infrastructure (P1)
   319→1. Create channel data model and storage (JSONL)
   320→2. Implement channels route with CRUD + message posting
   321→3. Auto-create team channels from existing teams
   322→4. Auto-create `#general` on first access
   323→
   324→### Phase 2 — Agent MCP tools (P1)
   325→1. Add `send_message` and `read_messages` MCP tools
   326→2. Add unread message hint to AgentRunner prompt injection
   327→3. Test with manual agent runs
   328→
   329→### Phase 3 — Client Chat tab (P1)
   330→1. Add Chat tab with channel list sidebar
   331→2. Message timeline view with sender, timestamp, text
   332→3. Thread collapse/expand
   333→4. Chairman message input
   334→5. Real-time updates via WebSocket
   335→
   336→### Phase 4 — Slack bridge (P3, future)
   337→1. Slack app integration (OAuth)
   338→2. Channel mapping: ClawCorp channel ↔ Slack channel
   339→3. Bidirectional message sync
   340→4. Slack reply → ClawCorp message
   341→
   342→---
   343→
   344→## Relationship to Existing Systems
   345→
   346→### What stays the same
   347→
   348→| System | Role | Still Used For |
   349→|---|---|---|
   350→| `escalate` MCP tool | Blocking question to supervisor | Agent is stuck, needs answer before continuing |
   351→| `report` MCP tool | Task completion report | Agent finishes mission, sends summary |
   352→| `delegate` MCP tool | Assign work to subordinate | Manager spawns sub-mission for subordinate |
   353→| Inbox tab | Chairman answers escalations | Viewing and responding to escalations |
   354→
   355→### What `send_message` adds
   356→
   357→| Use Case | Before (workaround) | After |
   358→|---|---|---|
   359→| FYI to teammate | No mechanism (lost context) | `send_message(to: "@qa-engineer", text: "...")` |
   360→| Team announcement | No mechanism | `send_message(to: "#engineering", text: "...")` |
   361→| Ask peer a question | `escalate` (wrong semantics, blocks agent) | `send_message` (non-blocking, no hierarchy required) |
   362→| Coordinate on shared work | Rely on mission descriptions | Real-time channel discussion |
   363→| Chairman gives informal feedback | Create a new mission (overkill) | DM or channel message |
   364→
   365→### Future: Slack Bridge Architecture (P3)
   366→
   367→```
   368→Agent calls send_message("#engineering", "Refactored auth module")
   369→    ↓
   370→ClawCorp channels route saves message
   371→    ↓
   372→If Slack bridge enabled:
   373→    ↓
   374→POST to Slack API → message appears in #clawcorp-engineering
   375→    ↓
   376→Someone replies in Slack
   377→    ↓
   378→Slack Event API webhook → ClawCorp channels route
   379→    ↓
   380→Message saved with from: "slack:username"
   381→    ↓
   382→Agent reads it on next run via read_messages()
   383→```
   384→
   385→---
   386→
   387→## Success Criteria
   388→
   389→- **SC-001**: Agents can send and read messages to/from any agent or channel
   390→- **SC-002**: Chairman can browse all channels and read all conversations
   391→- **SC-003**: Messages persist across server restarts and agent runs
   392→- **SC-004**: Unread message count is shown to agents on their next run
   393→- **SC-005**: Thread replies are visually grouped in the UI
   394→- **SC-006**: Chat tab loads within 1 second with 500+ messages across channels
   395→- **SC-007**: Data model is compatible with future Slack bridge integration
   396→

content.content:
1→import { Hono } from 'hono'
     2→import { runAgent, killAgent, isRunning, getRunningAgents } from '../lib/AgentRunner'
     3→import { readFile, writeFile, mkdir } from 'fs/promises'
     4→import { join } from 'path'
     5→import { existsSync } from 'fs'
     6→
     7→export const runRoutes = new Hono()
     8→
     9→const MISSIONS_DIR = join(import.meta.dir, '../../../missions')
    10→
    11→runRoutes.get('/', (c) => {
    12→  return c.json(getRunningAgents())
    13→})
    14→
    15→runRoutes.post('/', async (c) => {
    16→  const body = await c.req.json()
    17→  const { agentId, missionId, prompt, workspace, resume, sessionId } = body
    18→
    19→  if (!agentId || !missionId || !prompt) {
    20→    return c.json({ error: 'agentId, missionId, prompt required' }, 400)
    21→  }
    22→
    23→  if (isRunning(agentId)) {
    24→    return c.json({ error: `Agent ${agentId} is already busy` }, 409)
    25→  }
    26→
    27→  // Update mission state
    28→  const statePath = join(MISSIONS_DIR, missionId, 'state.json')
    29→  if (existsSync(statePath)) {
    30→    try {
    31→      const state = JSON.parse(await readFile(statePath, 'utf-8'))
    32→      state.status = 'in_progress'
    33→      state.current_stage = 'development'
    34→      state.assignee = agentId
    35→      if (workspace) state.workspace = workspace
    36→      state.history = state.history ?? []
    37→      state.history.push({ at: new Date().toISOString(), event: 'started', agentId, workspace })
    38→      await writeFile(statePath, JSON.stringify(state, null, 2))
    39→    } catch {}
    40→  }
    41→
    42→  runAgent(agentId, missionId, prompt, { workspace, resume, sessionId }).catch((err) => {
    43→    console.error(`Agent ${agentId} error:`, err.message)
    44→  })
    45→
    46→  return c.json({ ok: true, agentId, missionId, workspace })
    47→})
    48→
    49→runRoutes.delete('/:agentId', (c) => {
    50→  const agentId = c.req.param('agentId')
    51→  killAgent(agentId)
    52→  return c.json({ ok: true })
    53→})
    54→

content.content:
1→import { Hono } from 'hono'
     2→import { readdir, readFile, writeFile, mkdir } from 'fs/promises'
     3→import { join } from 'path'
     4→import { existsSync } from 'fs'
     5→import { broadcast } from '../lib/hub'
     6→
     7→export const messageRoutes = new Hono()
     8→
     9→const MISSIONS_DIR = join(import.meta.dir, '../../../missions')
    10→
    11→let msgCounter = 0
    12→
    13→function msgId() {
    14→  return `MSG-${Date.now()}-${++msgCounter}`
    15→}
    16→
    17→// GET /api/messages?missionId=&status=&to=
    18→messageRoutes.get('/', async (c) => {
    19→  const { missionId, status, to } = c.req.query()
    20→  const results: unknown[] = []
    21→
    22→  if (!existsSync(MISSIONS_DIR)) return c.json([])
    23→  const dirs = await readdir(MISSIONS_DIR, { withFileTypes: true })
    24→
    25→  for (const d of dirs.filter((d) => d.isDirectory())) {
    26→    if (missionId && d.name !== missionId) continue
    27→    const msgDir = join(MISSIONS_DIR, d.name, 'messages')
    28→    if (!existsSync(msgDir)) continue
    29→    const files = await readdir(msgDir).catch(() => [])
    30→    for (const f of files.filter((f) => f.endsWith('.json'))) {
    31→      const msg = JSON.parse(await readFile(join(msgDir, f), 'utf-8'))
    32→      if (status && msg.status !== status) continue
    33→      if (to && msg.to !== to) continue
    34→      results.push(msg)
    35→    }
    36→  }
    37→
    38→  return c.json(results)
    39→})
    40→
    41→// GET /api/messages/:id
    42→messageRoutes.get('/:id', async (c) => {
    43→  const id = c.req.param('id')
    44→  if (!existsSync(MISSIONS_DIR)) return c.json({ error: 'not found' }, 404)
    45→  const dirs = await readdir(MISSIONS_DIR, { withFileTypes: true })
    46→
    47→  for (const d of dirs.filter((d) => d.isDirectory())) {
    48→    const path = join(MISSIONS_DIR, d.name, 'messages', `${id}.json`)
    49→    if (existsSync(path)) {
    50→      return c.json(JSON.parse(await readFile(path, 'utf-8')))
    51→    }
    52→  }
    53→  return c.json({ error: 'not found' }, 404)
    54→})
    55→
    56→// POST /api/messages — create escalation or report
    57→messageRoutes.post('/', async (c) => {
    58→  const body = await c.req.json()
    59→  const { from, missionId, type, question, context, summary, artifacts } = body
    60→
    61→  // Resolve supervisor
    62→  const AGENTS_DIR = join(import.meta.dir, '../../../agents')
    63→  let to = 'chairman'
    64→  try {
    65→    const profile = JSON.parse(await readFile(join(AGENTS_DIR, from, 'profile.json'), 'utf-8'))
    66→    to = profile.reports_to ?? 'chairman'
    67→  } catch {}
    68→
    69→  const id = msgId()
    70→  const msg = {
    71→    id,
    72→    from,
    73→    to,
    74→    missionId,
    75→    type,
    76→    question: question ?? null,
    77→    context: context ?? null,
    78→    summary: summary ?? null,
    79→    artifacts: artifacts ?? [],
    80→    status: type === 'report' ? 'done' : 'pending',
    81→    answer: null,
    82→    created_at: new Date().toISOString(),
    83→    answered_at: null,
    84→  }
    85→
    86→  const msgDir = join(MISSIONS_DIR, missionId, 'messages')
    87→  await mkdir(msgDir, { recursive: true })
    88→  await writeFile(join(msgDir, `${id}.json`), JSON.stringify(msg, null, 2))
    89→
    90→  broadcast('message:new', msg)
    91→
    92→  // Auto-update mission state if report
    93→  if (type === 'report') {
    94→    const statePath = join(MISSIONS_DIR, missionId, 'state.json')
    95→    if (existsSync(statePath)) {
    96→      const state = JSON.parse(await readFile(statePath, 'utf-8'))
    97→      state.status = 'done'
    98→      state.current_stage = 'done'
    99→      state.history.push({ at: new Date().toISOString(), event: 'reported', summary })
   100→      await writeFile(statePath, JSON.stringify(state, null, 2))
   101→      broadcast('mission:done', { missionId, summary })
   102→    }
   103→  }
   104→
   105→  return c.json(msg, 201)
   106→})
   107→
   108→// POST /api/messages/:id/answer — Chairman or supervisor answers
   109→messageRoutes.post('/:id/answer', async (c) => {
   110→  const id = c.req.param('id')
   111→  const { answer } = await c.req.json()
   112→
   113→  if (!existsSync(MISSIONS_DIR)) return c.json({ error: 'not found' }, 404)
   114→  const dirs = await readdir(MISSIONS_DIR, { withFileTypes: true })
   115→
   116→  for (const d of dirs.filter((d) => d.isDirectory())) {
   117→    const path = join(MISSIONS_DIR, d.name, 'messages', `${id}.json`)
   118→    if (existsSync(path)) {
   119→      const msg = JSON.parse(await readFile(path, 'utf-8'))
   120→      msg.answer = answer
   121→      msg.status = 'answered'
   122→      msg.answered_at = new Date().toISOString()
   123→      await writeFile(path, JSON.stringify(msg, null, 2))
   124→      broadcast('message:answered', msg)
   125→      return c.json(msg)
   126→    }
   127→  }
   128→
   129→  return c.json({ error: 'not found' }, 404)
   130→})
   131→

Explore the ClawCorp codebase to understand the structure. I need to know:

1. The server structure - especially `server/src/routes/hire.ts`, `server/src/lib/` directory, how agents are created, how WebSocket broadcasting works
2. The client structure - especially `client/src/App.tsx`, how the hire modal works currently, how Live Log and WebSocket events are handled
3. `server/package.json` - current dependencies
4. Any existing patterns for spawning claude CLI (AgentRunner or similar)

Be very thorough - read full file contents for all relevant files. I need exact code patterns to build on.

content.text:
Perfect! I have gathered all the necessary information. Let me create a comprehensive exploration report: --- # ClawCorp Codebase Exploration Report ## Overview ClawCorp is an autonomous AI organization platform built with Bun runtime, Hono web framework, and Claude Code as agent drivers. The system orchestrates multi-agent collaboration with hierarchical structure, WebSocket-based real-time updates, and MCP (Model Context Protocol) integration for agent capabilities. --- ## 1. Server Structure ### 1.1 Core Server (`server/src/index.ts`) - **Framework**: Hono (lightweight web framework) - **Transport**: Bun built-in HTTP server with native WebSocket support - **Port**: 3001 - **CORS**: Configured for `http://localhost:5173` (client) - **Architecture**: Modular routes registered via `app.route()` **Route Structure**: ``` /api/agents → agent management /api/missions → mission creation & updates /api/run → agent execution (fire and capture output) /api/messages → escalations and reports /api/delegate → hierarchical delegation /api/hire → agent creation and smart hiring /api/tasks → task board management /api/teams → team organization ``` **WebSocket Handling**: - Native Bun server upgrade on `upgrade: 'websocket'` header - Callbacks: `open(ws)` → add to hub, `close(ws)` → remove, `message()` → no-op (server only broadcasts) ### 1.2 Hub System (`server/src/lib/hub.ts`) **Simple but effective broadcast pattern**: ```typescript const clients = new Set() export function broadcast(event: string, data: unknown) { const msg = JSON.stringify({ event, data, ts: Date.now() }) for (const ws of clients) { if (ws.readyState === 1) ws.send(msg) } } ``` **Events used**: - `agent:start`, `agent:output`, `agent:error`, `agent:done`, `agent:killed` - `message:new`, `message:answered` - `mission:done`, `mission:updated` - `task:created`, `task:updated`, `task:deleted` - `hire:start`, `hire:candidates`, `hire:interview_start`, `hire:interview_done`, `hire:scores`, `hire:complete`, `hire:error` - `team:created`, `team:updated`, `team:deleted` ### 1.3 Routes in Detail #### `/api/hire` (`server/src/routes/hire.ts`) **POST /api/hire** — Create agent with explicit profile ```typescript Body: { id, title, department, description, reports_to, system_prompt, cost_model, rank, team } Creates: - agents/{id}/profile.json - agents/{id}/memory.md Response: profile (201) ``` **POST /api/hire/smart** — Smart hiring (async) ```typescript Body: { description: string } Workflow: 1. Fire-and-forget via runSmartHire() 2. Broadcast events via WebSocket (hire:start, hire:candidates, hire:interview_*, hire:scores, hire:complete) 3. Returns: { ok: true, hireId } ``` **DELETE /api/hire/:id** — Fire/archive agent ```typescript Moves agents/{id} → archive/{id}-{timestamp} Response: { ok: true, archived: path } ``` #### `/api/run` (`server/src/routes/run.ts`) **GET /api/run** — Get list of running agent IDs **POST /api/run** — Start agent execution ```typescript Body: { agentId, missionId, prompt, workspace?, resume?, sessionId? } Updates mission state: status=in_progress, current_stage=development Calls: runAgent(agentId, missionId, prompt, options) Response: { ok: true, agentId, missionId, workspace } ``` **DELETE /api/run/:agentId** — Kill agent process ### 1.4 AgentRunner (`server/src/lib/AgentRunner.ts`) **Key Function**: `runAgent(agentId, missionId, prompt, options)` **Driver Types Supported**: 1. `claude-code` — Default, spawns `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions -p {prompt}` 2. `cli` — Legacy `opencode` style 3. Fallback to `claude` CLI **Key Patterns**: 1. **Prompt Injection**: ```typescript fullPrompt = `${systemPrompt}\n\n---\nMission ID: ${missionId}\nArtifacts directory: ${artifactsDir}\nSave ALL output files to the artifacts directory above.\nTask: ${prompt}` ``` 2. **Environment Setup**: - Normalizes `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN` → `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` - **Deletes** `CLAUDECODE` and `CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT` env vars so nested agents can spawn claude CLI 3. **MCP Config Injection** (`injectMcpConfig`): ```typescript .claude/settings.json is created/merged with: { mcpServers: { clawcorp: { command: 'bun', args: ['run', MCP_SERVER_PATH], env: { CLAWCORP_SERVER: 'http://localhost:3001', CLAWCORP_AGENT_ID: agentId, CLAWCORP_MISSION_ID: missionId, CLAWCORP_AGENTS_DIR: AGENTS_DIR } } } } ``` 4. **Process Spawning**: ```typescript const proc = Bun.spawn(cmd, { cwd, stdout: 'pipe', stderr: 'pipe', env: { ... } }) ``` 5. **Stream Handling**: - Async loop on `proc.stdout` and `proc.stderr` - Broadcasts via `broadcast('agent:output', ...)` and `broadcast('agent:error', ...)` - Uses TextDecoder for UTF-8 chunks 6. **Session Persistence** (for opencode): ```typescript After exit, reads opencode session via: Bun.spawn(['opencode', 'session', 'list']) Stores in missions/{missionId}/state.json: sessions[agentId] = sessionId ``` ### 1.5 SmartHire (`server/src/lib/SmartHire.ts`) **Flow**: `runSmartHire(description, hireId)` **Phase 1 — Generate Candidates**: ```typescript Uses claude-sonnet-4-20250514 to generate 3 candidates Input: plain language description Output: JSON array with: id, title, department, description, reports_to, rank, team, system_prompt ``` **Phase 2 — Interview in Parallel**: ```typescript For each candidate: 1. runInterview(candidate) → Bun.spawn(['claude', '--dangerously-skip-permissions', '-p', prompt]) 2. Timeout: 60 seconds 3. Test tasks per department: - Engineering: "Write TypeScript isPalindrome(s: string): boolean with 3 test cases" - Research Lab: "List 5 open research questions..." - Product: "Write 3 Given/When/Then scenarios for user login" 4. Captures stdout as output ``` **Phase 3 — Score Candidates**: ```typescript Uses claude-sonnet-4-20250514 evaluator Rubric (0-10 scale): - task_completion: 40% - output_quality: 30% - autonomy: 20% - conciseness: 10% Weighted score = sum(score × weight) Tiebreak: simpler system_prompt wins ``` **Phase 4 — Hire Winner**: ```typescript Creates agents/{finalId}/ with profile.json and memory.md finalId = id if new, else id-{timestamp} if collision Broadcasts: broadcast('hire:complete', { hireId, agent: profile }) ``` **Phase 5 — Archive**: ```typescript Stores in archive/interviews/H-{hireId}.json: { hireId, description, timestamp, candidates: [{ ...profile, exitCode, output, scores }], winner: top_candidate_id } ``` ### 1.6 Message Routes (`server/src/routes/messages.ts`) **Data Structure**: ```typescript type Message = { id: string // MSG-{timestamp}-{counter} from: string // agent ID to: string // resolved from agent.reports_to missionId: string type: 'escalate' | 'report' question?: string // for escalate context?: string summary?: string // for report artifacts?: string[] status: 'pending' | 'answered' | 'done' answer?: string created_at: string answered_at?: string } ``` **Storage**: `missions/{missionId}/messages/{id}.json` **GET /api/messages** — List messages (with filters) ```typescript Query params: missionId?, status?, to? Scans all mission directories for messages/ ``` **POST /api/messages** — Create escalation/report ```typescript Auto-resolves supervisor from agent profile.reports_to Sets status = 'pending' for escalate, 'done' for report Broadcasts: broadcast('message:new', msg) If type='report': also broadcasts mission:done and updates mission state ``` **POST /api/messages/:id/answer** — Chairman answers escalation ```typescript Updates message: answer, status='answered', answered_at Broadcasts: broadcast('message:answered', msg) ``` ### 1.7 Package.json Dependencies ```json { "@anthropic-ai/sdk": "^0.39.0", "@hono/node-server": "^1.13.0", "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk": "^1.26.0", "hono": "^4.6.0" } ``` **Note**: Uses Bun (no node) but SDK imports suggest potential Node.js compatibility layer. ### 1.8 MCP Server (`server/src/mcp/server.ts`) **Transport**: stdio (MCP spec standard) **Environment Variables**: - `CLAWCORP_SERVER` → HTTP endpoint - `CLAWCORP_AGENT_ID` → current agent - `CLAWCORP_MISSION_ID` → current mission - `CLAWCORP_AGENTS_DIR` → agents directory **Available Tools**: 1. **escalate** — Blocks execution, polls for supervisor answer (10 min max) 2. **delegate** — Spawn sub-mission for subordinate 3. **report** — Signal task completion 4. **memory_read** — Read `agents/{id}/memory.md` 5. **memory_write** — Append to memory.md 6. **cross_team_delegate** — Director+ only, cross-department task handoff --- ## 2. Client Structure ### 2.1 Core App (`client/src/App.tsx`) **Main State**: ```typescript agents, missions, messages, logs, tasks, teams expandedDept, tab (tasks | inbox | agents) runTarget, selectedAgent, resumeSession showSmartHire, smartHireDesc, hiringInProgress answerTarget, answerText dragMission, dragTask, pushBackTarget, pushBackText ``` **Lifecycle**: ```typescript useEffect(() => { fetch('/api/agents').then() → agents + running status fetch('/api/run').then() → set status=running fetch('/api/missions').then() fetch('/api/messages').then() fetch('/api/tasks').then() fetch('/api/teams').then() }, []) ``` ### 2.2 WebSocket Hook (`client/src/hooks/useWebSocket.ts`) ```typescript export function useWebSocket(onMessage: (msg: WsMessage) => void) { const socket = new WebSocket('ws://localhost:3001/ws') socket.onmessage = (e) => { const msg: WsMessage = JSON.parse(e.data) // { event, data, ts } onMessageRef.current(msg) } socket.onclose = () => setTimeout(connect, 2000) // auto-reconnect } ``` ### 2.3 Event Handlers in App.tsx **Agent Events**: ```typescript 'agent:start' → set status=running, append log 'agent:output' → append log (kind='output') 'agent:error' → append log (kind='error') 'agent:done' → set status=idle, update mission (current_stage='done'), save sessionId 'agent:killed' → set status=idle, append log ``` **Message Events**: ```typescript 'message:new' → add to messages, append contextual log ``` **Mission Events**: ```typescript 'mission:done' → update current_stage='done' 'mission:updated' → sync with new data ``` **Task Events**: ```typescript 'task:created', 'task:updated', 'task:deleted' ``` **Smart Hire Events**: ```typescript 'hire:start' → hiringInProgress=true 'hire:candidates' → log candidate IDs 'hire:interview_start', 'hire:interview_done' → log per-candidate progress 'hire:scores' → log weighted scores 'hire:complete' → add new agent to agents[], hiringInProgress=false 'hire:error' → hiringInProgress=false, append error log ``` **Team Events**: ```typescript 'team:created', 'team:updated', 'team:deleted' ``` ### 2.4 UI Tabs #### Tab 1: Tasks - Kanban board: backlog → todo → in_progress → review → done - Drag-to-move tasks between columns - Push-back modal (move task back to in_progress with feedback) - Delete task option - Feedback history per task #### Tab 2: Inbox - Lists messages (escalations and reports) - Shows status: pending | answered | done - Modal to answer escalations - Displays artifacts and context - Message type badges (escalate | report) #### Tab 3: Agents - **Org Tree**: - Chairman (root) - C-suite executives (reports_to=chairman) - Teams under executives (collapsible dept tree) - Unassigned agents - **Agent Node**: - Status indicator (idle | running with animate-dot) - Title, rank badge, description - ID in monospace - "fire" button, "Kill session" button when running - Hierarchical nesting with connectors ### 2.5 Modals 1. **Run Mission**: - Select agent (disabled if status=running) - Resume session checkbox (conditional) - Workspace path input - Prompt textarea - Run button 2. **Smart Hire**: - Textarea for description - "Start Interview" button - Shows hiringInProgress spinner in header 3. **Answer Escalation**: - Shows question and context - Textarea for answer - Send button 4. **Push Back Task**: - Shows task title - Textarea for reason - Confirm button ### 2.6 Live Log (Sidebar) - Right sidebar (xl: 260px, collapsed on mobile) - Monospace font, auto-scroll to bottom - Three line kinds: system (blue), error (red), output (default) - System logs show agent ID - Clear button - Keeps last 500 lines in state --- ## 3. Data Structures & Storage ### 3.1 Agent Profile **File**: `agents/{id}/profile.json` ```json { "id": "string", "title": "string", "department": "Engineering" | "Research Lab" | "Product", "description": "string", "driver": { "type": "claude-code" | "cli", "command": "claude" | "opencode", "args": ["array", "of", "args"], "system_prompt": "string" }, "reports_to": "chairman" | "agent_id", "subordinates": ["agent_id"], "rank": "executive" | "director" | "lead" | "senior" | "member" | "intern", "team": "team_id", "cost_model": "low" | "medium" | "high" } ``` ### 3.2 Mission State **File**: `missions/{id}/state.json` ```json { "id": "M-{timestamp}", "title": "string", "type": "engineering" | "research", "status": "backlog" | "analysis" | "design" | "development" | "testing" | "done", "current_stage": "same as status", "assignee": "agent_id | null", "workspace": "path | null", "sessions": { "agent_id": "session_id" }, "created_at": "ISO string", "history": [ { "at": "ISO string", "event": "started" | "updated", "agentId": "...", ... } ] } ``` ### 3.3 Agent Memory **File**: `agents/{id}/memory.md` - Markdown format - Appended to on each `memory_write` MCP call - Timestamped entries ### 3.4 Mission Artifacts **Directory**: `missions/{missionId}/artifacts/` - Created by agents during runs - All output files should be saved here ### 3.5 Smart Hire Archive **File**: `archive/interviews/H-{hireId}.json` ```json { "hireId": "string", "description": "string", "timestamp": "ISO string", "candidates": [ { "id": "string", "title": "string", ...profile fields..., "exitCode": number, "output": "string", "scores": { "task_completion": number, "output_quality": number, "autonomy": number, "conciseness": number, "weighted": number } } ], "winner": "agent_id" } ``` --- ## 4. Hire Modal Implementation (Current) **Location**: `client/src/App.tsx` lines 1032–1070 **State**: ```typescript const [showSmartHire, setShowSmartHire] = useState(false) const [smartHireDesc, setSmartHireDesc] = useState('') const [hiringInProgress, setHiringInProgress] = useState(false) ``` **Trigger**: Button in agents tab header ```typescript ``` **Modal**: ```typescript {showSmartHire && (

Smart Hire

Client Structure ### 2.1 Core App (`client/src/App.tsx`) **Main State**: ```typescript agents, missions, messages, logs, tasks, teams expandedDept, tab (tasks | inbox | agents) runTarget, selectedAgent, resumeSession showSmartHire, smartHireDesc, hiringInProgress answerTarget, answerText dragMission, dragTask, pushBackTarget, pushBackText ``` **Lifecycle**: ```typescript useEffect(() => { fetch('/api/agents').then() → agents + running status fetch('/api/run').then() → set status=running fetch('/api/missions').then() fetch('/api/messages').then() fetch('/api/tasks').then() fetch('/api/teams').then() }, []) ``` ### 2.2 WebSocket Hook (`client/src/hooks/useWebSocket.ts`) ```typescript export function useWebSocket(onMessage: (msg: WsMessage) => void) { const socket = new WebSocket('ws://localhost:3001/ws') socket.onmessage = (e) => { const msg: WsMessage = JSON.parse(e.data) // { event, data, ts } onMessageRef.current(msg) } socket.onclose = () => setTimeout(connect, 2000) // auto-reconnect } ``` ### 2.3 Event Handlers in App.tsx **Agent Events**: ```typescript 'agent:start' → set status=running, append log 'agent:output' → append log (kind='output') 'agent:error' → append log (kind='error') 'agent:done' → set status=idle, update mission (current_stage='done'), save sessionId 'agent:killed' → set status=idle, append log ``` **Message Events**: ```typescript 'message:new' → add to messages, append contextual log ``` **Mission Events**: ```typescript 'mission:done' → update current_stage='done' 'mission:updated' → sync with new data ``` **Task Events**: ```typescript 'task:created', 'task:updated', 'task:deleted' ``` **Smart Hire Events**: ```typescript 'hire:start' → hiringInProgress=true 'hire:candidates' → log candidate IDs 'hire:interview_start', 'hire:interview_done' → log per-candidate progress 'hire:scores' → log weighted scores 'hire:complete' → add new agent to agents[], hiringInProgress=false 'hire:error' → hiringInProgress=false, append error log ``` **Team Events**: ```typescript 'team:created', 'team:updated', 'team:deleted' ``` ### 2.4 UI Tabs #### Tab 1: Tasks - Kanban board: backlog → todo → in_progress → review → done - Drag-to-move tasks between columns - Push-back modal (move task back to in_progress with feedback) - Delete task option - Feedback history per task #### Tab 2: Inbox - Lists messages (escalations and reports) - Shows status: pending | answered | done - Modal to answer escalations - Displays artifacts and context - Message type badges (escalate | report) #### Tab 3: Agents - **Org Tree**: - Chairman (root) - C-suite executives (reports_to=chairman) - Teams under executives (collapsible dept tree) - Unassigned agents - **Agent Node**: - Status indicator (idle | running with animate-dot) - Title, rank badge, description - ID in monospace - "fire" button, "Kill session" button when running - Hierarchical nesting with connectors ### 2.5 Modals 1. **Run Mission**: - Select agent (disabled if status=running) - Resume session checkbox (conditional) - Workspace path input - Prompt textarea - Run button 2. **Smart Hire**: - Textarea for description - "Start Interview" button - Shows hiringInProgress spinner in header 3. **Answer Escalation**: - Shows question and context - Textarea for answer - Send button 4. **Push Back Task**: - Shows task title - Textarea for reason - Confirm button ### 2.6 Live Log (Sidebar) - Right sidebar (xl: 260px, collapsed on mobile) - Monospace font, auto-scroll to bottom - Three line kinds: system (blue), error (red), output (default) - System logs show agent ID - Clear button - Keeps last 500 lines in state --- ## 3. Data Structures & Storage ### 3.1 Agent Profile **File**: `agents/{id}/profile.json` ```json { "id": "string", "title": "string", "department": "Engineering" | "Research Lab" | "Product", "description": "string", "driver": { "type": "claude-code" | "cli", "command": "claude" | "opencode", "args": ["array", "of", "args"], "system_prompt": "string" }, "reports_to": "chairman" | "agent_id", "subordinates": ["agent_id"], "rank": "executive" | "director" | "lead" | "senior" | "member" | "intern", "team": "team_id", "cost_model": "low" | "medium" | "high" } ``` ### 3.2 Mission State **File**: `missions/{id}/state.json` ```json { "id": "M-{timestamp}", "title": "string", "type": "engineering" | "research", "status": "backlog" | "analysis" | "design" | "development" | "testing" | "done", "current_stage": "same as status", "assignee": "agent_id | null", "workspace": "path | null", "sessions": { "agent_id": "session_id" }, "created_at": "ISO string", "history": [ { "at": "ISO string", "event": "started" | "updated", "agentId": "...", ... } ] } ``` ### 3.3 Agent Memory **File**: `agents/{id}/memory.md` - Markdown format - Appended to on each `memory_write` MCP call - Timestamped entries ### 3.4 Mission Artifacts **Directory**: `missions/{missionId}/artifacts/` - Created by agents during runs - All output files should be saved here ### 3.5 Smart Hire Archive **File**: `archive/interviews/H-{hireId}.json` ```json { "hireId": "string", "description": "string", "timestamp": "ISO string", "candidates": [ { "id": "string", "title": "string", ...profile fields..., "exitCode": number, "output": "string", "scores": { "task_completion": number, "output_quality": number, "autonomy": number, "conciseness": number, "weighted": number } } ], "winner": "agent_id" } ``` --- ## 4. 1→import Anthropic from '@anthropic-ai/sdk'
     2→import { writeFile, mkdir } from 'fs/promises'

     4→import { existsSync } from 'fs'
     5→import { broadcast } from './hub'

    10→function inferTeamId(department: string): string {
    11→  const map: Record<string, string> = {
    12→    'Engineering': 'engineering',
    13→    'Research Lab': 'research-lab',
    14→    'Product': 'product',
    15→  }
    16→  return map[department] ?? 'engineering'
    17→}
    18→
    19→const TEST_TASKS: Record<string, string> = {
    20→  Engineering:
    21→    'Write a TypeScript function isPalindrome(s: string): boolean with 3 test cases',
    22→  'Research Lab':
    23→    'List 5 open research questions in the field of autonomous AI agents',
    24→  Product:
    25→    'Write 3 Given/When/Then acceptance scenarios for a user login feature',
    26→}
    27→
    28→type Candidate = {
    29→  id: string
    30→  title: string
    31→  department: string
    32→  description: string
    33→  reports_to: string
    34→  system_prompt: string
    35→  rank?: string
    36→  team?: string
    37→}
    38→
    39→type InterviewResult = {
    40→  candidate: Candidate
    41→  output: string
    42→  exitCode: number
    43→  scores?: {
    44→    task_completion: number
    45→    output_quality: number
    46→    autonomy: number
    47→    conciseness: number
    48→    weighted: number
    49→  }
    50→}
    51→
    52→export async function runSmartHire(description: string, hireId: string) {
    53→  const anthropic = new Anthropic()
    54→
    55→  try {
    56→    broadcast('hire:start', { hireId, description })
    57→
    58→    // Phase 1: Generate candidates
    59→    const candidates = await generateCandidates(anthropic, description)
    60→    broadcast('hire:candidates', {
    61→      hireId,
    62→      candidates: candidates.map((c) => ({ id: c.id, title: c.title })),
    63→    })
    64→
    65→    // Phase 2: Run interviews in parallel
    66→    const results: InterviewResult[] = []
    67→    const interviews = candidates.map(async (candidate) => {
    68→      broadcast('hire:interview_start', { hireId, candidateId: candidate.id })
    69→      const result = await runInterview(candidate)
    70→      broadcast('hire:interview_done', {
    71→        hireId,
    72→        candidateId: candidate.id,
    73→        exitCode: result.exitCode,
    74→      })
    75→      return result
    76→    })
    77→
    78→    const interviewResults = await Promise.all(interviews)
    79→    results.push(...interviewResults)
    80→
    81→    // Phase 3: Score candidates
    82→    const scored = await scoreCandidates(anthropic, description, results)
    83→    broadcast('hire:scores', {
    84→      hireId,
    85→      scores: scored.map((r) => ({
    86→        candidateId: r.candidate.id,
    87→        weighted: r.scores!.weighted,
    88→      })),
    89→    })
    90→
    91→    // Find winner (highest weighted score; tiebreak: shorter system_prompt)
    92→    const sorted = [...scored]
    93→      .filter((r) => r.scores)
    94→      .sort((a, b) => {
    95→        const diff = b.scores!.weighted - a.scores!.weighted
    96→        if (Math.abs(diff) > 0.01) return diff
    97→        return a.candidate.system_prompt.length - b.candidate.system_prompt.length
    98→      })
    99→
   100→    if (sorted.length === 0) {
   101→      broadcast('hire:error', { hireId, message: 'All candidates failed scoring' })
   102→      return
   103→    }
   104→
   105→    const winner = sorted[0]
   106→
   107→    // Phase 4: Hire winner
   108→    const profile = await hireWinner(winner.candidate)
   109→    broadcast('hire:complete', { hireId, agent: profile })
   110→
   111→    // Phase 5: Archive results
   112→    await archiveResults(hireId, description, results)
   113→  } catch (err: unknown) {
   114→    const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)
   115→    broadcast('hire:error', { hireId, message })
   116→  }
   117→}
   118→
   119→async function generateCandidates(
   120→  anthropic: Anthropic,
   121→  description: string
   122→): Promise<Candidate[]> {
   123→  const response = await anthropic.messages.create({
   124→    model: 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514',
   125→    max_tokens: 2048,
   126→    messages: [
   127→      {
   128→        role: 'user',
   129→        content: `You are a hiring manager at ClawCorp, an autonomous AI organization.
   130→
   131→The Chairman needs: "${description}"
   132→
   133→Generate 3 distinct agent profiles in JSON array format. Each should have:
   134→- id: a short kebab-case slug (e.g. "sql-optimizer")
   135→- title: a human-readable job title
   136→- department: one of "Engineering", "Research Lab", or "Product"
   137→- description: one sentence describing this agent's specialty
   138→- reports_to: infer from department — "principal-investigator" for Research Lab, "architect" for Engineering, "product-manager" for Product
   139→- rank: one of "senior", "member", or "intern" based on the complexity/seniority implied
   140→- team: the team id — "engineering", "research-lab", or "product"
   141→- system_prompt: specific, actionable instructions including the 3 mandatory rules:
   142→  1. Never ask clarifying questions — make assumptions and proceed
   143→  2. Use escalate tool if blocked
   144→  3. Use report tool when done
   145→
   146→Each candidate should have a different specialization angle or prompting strategy.
   147→Return ONLY a JSON array, no explanation or markdown fences.`,
   148→      },
   149→    ],
   150→  })
   151→
   152→  const text =
   153→    response.content[0].type === 'text' ? response.content[0].text : ''
   154→  // Extract JSON array from response (handle possible markdown fences)
   155→  const jsonMatch = text.match(/\[[\s\S]*\]/)
   156→  if (!jsonMatch) throw new Error('Failed to parse candidates from LLM response')
   157→  return JSON.parse(jsonMatch[0])
   158→}
   159→
   160→async function runInterview(candidate: Candidate): Promise<InterviewResult> {
   161→  const testTask =
   162→    TEST_TASKS[candidate.department] ?? TEST_TASKS['Engineering']
   163→
   164→  const prompt = `${candidate.system_prompt}\n\nTASK: ${testTask}`
   165→
   166→  try {
   167→    const proc = Bun.spawn(
   168→      ['claude', '--dangerously-skip-permissions', '-p', prompt],
   169→      {
   170→        cwd: '/tmp',
   171→        stdout: 'pipe',
   172→        stderr: 'pipe',
   173→        env: {
   174→          ...process.env,
   175→          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY:
   176→            process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY ??
   177→            process.env.ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN ??
   178→            '',
   179→          CLAUDECODE: undefined as unknown as string,
   180→          CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT: undefined as unknown as string,
   181→        },
   182→      }
   183→    )
   184→
   185→    // 60s timeout
   186→    const timeout = setTimeout(() => proc.kill(), 60_000)
   187→
   188→    const output = await new Response(proc.stdout).text()
   189→    const exitCode = await proc.exited
   190→    clearTimeout(timeout)
   191→
   192→    return { candidate, output, exitCode }
   193→  } catch {
   194→    return { candidate, output: '', exitCode: 1 }
   195→  }
   196→}
   197→
   198→async function scoreCandidates(
   199→  anthropic: Anthropic,
   200→  description: string,
   201→  results: InterviewResult[]
   202→): Promise<InterviewResult[]> {
   203→  const candidateSummaries = results
   204→    .map(
   205→      (r, i) =>
   206→        `## Candidate ${i + 1}: ${r.candidate.title} (${r.candidate.id})
   207→Department: ${r.candidate.department}
   208→Description: ${r.candidate.description}
   209→Exit Code: ${r.exitCode}
   210→
   211→### Test Output:
   212→${r.output.slice(0, 3000)}
   213→`
   214→    )
   215→    .join('\n---\n')
   216→
   217→  const response = await anthropic.messages.create({
   218→    model: 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514',
   219→    max_tokens: 1024,
   220→    messages: [
   221→      {
   222→        role: 'user',
   223→        content: `You are evaluating candidates for: "${description}"
   224→
   225→${candidateSummaries}
   226→
   227→Score each candidate 0-10 on these dimensions:
   228→- task_completion (40%): Did they actually do what was asked?
   229→- output_quality (30%): Is the output correct, precise, useful?
   230→- autonomy (20%): Did they make decisions without asking questions?
   231→- conciseness (10%): Did they avoid unnecessary verbosity?
   232→
   233→Return ONLY a JSON array of objects with candidateId and the 4 scores. No explanation.
   234→Example: [{"candidateId": "foo", "task_completion": 8, "output_quality": 7, "autonomy": 9, "conciseness": 8}]`,
   235→      },
   236→    ],
   237→  })
   238→
   239→  const text =
   240→    response.content[0].type === 'text' ? response.content[0].text : ''
   241→  const jsonMatch = text.match(/\[[\s\S]*\]/)
   242→  if (!jsonMatch) return results
   243→
   244→  const scores: Array<{
   245→    candidateId: string
   246→    task_completion: number
   247→    output_quality: number
   248→    autonomy: number
   249→    conciseness: number
   250→  }> = JSON.parse(jsonMatch[0])
   251→
   252→  return results.map((r) => {
   253→    const s = scores.find((sc) => sc.candidateId === r.candidate.id)
   254→    if (!s) return r
   255→    const weighted =
   256→      s.task_completion * 0.4 +
   257→      s.output_quality * 0.3 +
   258→      s.autonomy * 0.2 +
   259→      s.conciseness * 0.1
   260→    return {
   261→      ...r,
   262→      scores: { ...s, weighted: Math.round(weighted * 100) / 100 },
   263→    }
   264→  })
   265→}
   266→
   267→async function hireWinner(candidate: Candidate) {
   268→  const agentDir = join(AGENTS_DIR, candidate.id)
   269→
   270→  // Avoid collision — append timestamp if exists
   271→  const finalId = existsSync(agentDir)
   272→    ? `${candidate.id}-${Date.now()}`
   273→    : candidate.id
   274→  const finalDir = join(AGENTS_DIR, finalId)
   275→
   276→  await mkdir(finalDir, { recursive: true })
   277→
   278→  const profile = {
   279→    id: finalId,
   280→    title: candidate.title,
   281→    department: candidate.department,
   282→    description: candidate.description,
   283→    driver: {
   284→      type: 'claude-code',
   285→      command: 'claude',
   286→      args: ['--dangerously-skip-permissions', '-p', '{{full_prompt}}'],
   287→      system_prompt: candidate.system_prompt,
   288→    },
   289→    reports_to: candidate.reports_to,
   290→    subordinates: [],
   291→    rank: candidate.rank ?? 'member',
   292→    team: candidate.team ?? inferTeamId(candidate.department),
   293→    cost_model: 'medium',
   294→  }
   295→
   296→  await writeFile(join(finalDir, 'profile.json'), JSON.stringify(profile, null, 2))
   297→  await writeFile(join(finalDir, 'memory.md'), `# ${candidate.title} Memory\n\n`)
   298→
   299→  return profile
   300→}
   301→
   302→async function archiveResults(
   303→  hireId: string,
   304→  description: string,
   305→  results: InterviewResult[]
   306→) {
   307→  const dir = join(ARCHIVE_DIR, 'interviews')
   308→  await mkdir(dir, { recursive: true })
   309→
   310→  const record = {
   311→    hireId,
   312→    description,
   313→    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
   314→    candidates: results.map((r) => ({
   315→      ...r.candidate,
   316→      exitCode: r.exitCode,
   317→      output: r.output,
   318→      scores: r.scores,
   319→    })),
   320→    winner: results
   321→      .filter((r) => r.scores)
   322→      .sort((a, b) => (b.scores?.weighted ?? 0) - (a.scores?.weighted ?? 0))[0]
   323→      ?.candidate.id,
   324→  }
   325→
   326→  await writeFile(
   327→    join(dir, `H-${hireId}.json`),
   328→    JSON.stringify(record, null, 2)
   329→  )
   330→}

1→import { Hono } from 'hono'
     2→import { readFile, writeFile, mkdir, rename } from 'fs/promises'
     3→import { join } from 'path'
     4→import { existsSync } from 'fs'
     5→import { runSmartHire } from '../lib/SmartHire'
     6→
     7→export const hireRoutes = new Hono()
     8→
     9→const AGENTS_DIR = join(import.meta.dir, '../../../agents')
    10→const ARCHIVE_DIR = join(import.meta.dir, '../../../archive')
    11→
    12→// POST /api/hire — create new agent
    13→hireRoutes.post('/', async (c) => {
    14→  const body = await c.req.json()
    15→  const { id, title, department, description, reports_to, system_prompt, cost_model, rank, team } = body
    16→
    17→  if (!id || !title) return c.json({ error: 'id and title required' }, 400)
    18→
    19→  const agentDir = join(AGENTS_DIR, id)
    20→  if (existsSync(agentDir)) return c.json({ error: `Agent ${id} already exists` }, 409)
    21→
    22→  await mkdir(agentDir, { recursive: true })
    23→
    24→  const profile = {
    25→    id,
    26→    title,
    27→    department: department ?? 'Engineering',
    28→    description: description ?? '',
    29→    driver: {
    30→      type: 'claude-code',
    31→      command: 'claude',
    32→      args: ['--dangerously-skip-permissions', '-p', '{{full_prompt}}'],
    33→      system_prompt: system_prompt ?? `You are ${title} at ClawCorp.\n\nRULES:\n1. Never ask questions. Make assumptions and proceed.\n2. Use escalate tool if blocked.\n3. Use report tool when done.`,
    34→    },
    35→    reports_to: reports_to ?? 'chairman',
    36→    subordinates: [],
    37→    rank: rank ?? 'member',
    38→    team: team ?? '',
    39→    cost_model: cost_model ?? 'medium',
    40→  }
    41→
    42→  await writeFile(join(agentDir, 'profile.json'), JSON.stringify(profile, null, 2))
    43→  await writeFile(join(agentDir, 'memory.md'), `# ${title} Memory\n\n`)
    44→
    45→  return c.json(profile, 201)
    46→})
    47→
    48→// POST /api/hire/smart — smart hire via natural language
    49→hireRoutes.post('/smart', async (c) => {
    50→  const body = await c.req.json()
    51→  const { description } = body
    52→
    53→  if (!description?.trim()) return c.json({ error: 'description required' }, 400)
    54→
    55→  const hireId = `${Date.now()}`
    56→  // Fire and forget — progress streams via WebSocket
    57→  runSmartHire(description.trim(), hireId)
    58→
    59→  return c.json({ ok: true, hireId })
    60→})
    61→
    62→// DELETE /api/hire/:id — fire (archive) agent
    63→hireRoutes.delete('/:id', async (c) => {
    64→  const id = c.req.param('id')
    65→  const agentDir = join(AGENTS_DIR, id)
    66→
    67→  if (!existsSync(agentDir)) return c.json({ error: 'Agent not found' }, 404)
    68→
    69→  await mkdir(ARCHIVE_DIR, { recursive: true })
    70→  const archivePath = join(ARCHIVE_DIR, `${id}-${Date.now()}`)
    71→  await rename(agentDir, archivePath)
    72→
    73→  return c.json({ ok: true, archived: archivePath })
    74→})

   171→function formatMessageTime(ts: string) {
   172→  const date = new Date(ts)
   173→  if (Number.isNaN(date.getTime())) return ts
   174→  return date.toLocaleString([], { month: 'short', day: 'numeric', hour: '2-digit', minute: '2-digit' })

   176→
   177→export default function App() {
   178→  const [tab, setTab] = useState<'tasks' | 'inbox' | 'agents'>('tasks')
   179→  const [agents, setAgents] = useState<Agent[]>([])
   180→  const [missions, setMissions] = useState<Mission[]>([])
   181→  const [messages, setMessages] = useState<Message[]>([])
   182→  const [logs, setLogs] = useState<LogLine[]>([])
   183→  const [tasks, setTasks] = useState<Task[]>([])
   184→  const [teams, setTeams] = useState<Team[]>([])
   185→  const [expandedDept, setExpandedDept] = useState<string | null>(null)
   186→  const [newTitle, setNewTitle] = useState('')
   187→  const [showQuickAdd, setShowQuickAdd] = useState(false)
   188→  const [newTaskTitle, setNewTaskTitle] = useState('')
   189→
   190→  const [runTarget, setRunTarget] = useState<{
   191→    missionId: string
   192→    prompt: string
   193→    workspace: string
   194→    sessions: Record<string, string>
   195→  } | null>(null)
   196→  const [selectedAgent, setSelectedAgent] = useState('')
   197→  const [resumeSession, setResumeSession] = useState(false)
   198→
   199→  const [showSmartHire, setShowSmartHire] = useState(false)
   200→  const [smartHireDesc, setSmartHireDesc] = useState('')
   201→  const [hiringInProgress, setHiringInProgress] = useState(false)
   202→
   203→  const [answerTarget, setAnswerTarget] = useState<Message | null>(null)
   204→  const [answerText, setAnswerText] = useState('')
   205→
   206→  const [dragMission, setDragMission] = useState<string | null>(null)
   207→  const [dragTask, setDragTask] = useState<string | null>(null)
   208→  const [pushBackTarget, setPushBackTarget] = useState<Task | null>(null)
   209→  const [pushBackText, setPushBackText] = useState('')
   210→
   211→  const logScrollRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null)
   212→
   213→  useEffect(() => {
   214→    fetch('/api/agents')
   215→      .then((r) => r.json())
   216→      .then((d) => setAgents(d.map((a: Agent) => ({ ...a, status: 'idle' }))))
   217→      .then(() =>
   218→        fetch('/api/run')
   219→          .then((r) => r.json())
   220→          .then((running: string[]) => {
   221→            if (running.length > 0)
   222→              setAgents((p) => p.map((a) => (running.includes(a.id) ? { ...a, status: 'running' } : a)))
   223→          })
   224→      )
   225→      .catch(console.error)
   226→
   227→    fetch('/api/missions').then((r) => r.json()).then(setMissions).catch(console.error)
   228→    fetch('/api/messages').then((r) => r.json()).then(setMessages).catch(console.error)
   229→    fetch('/api/tasks').then((r) => r.json()).then(setTasks).catch(console.error)
   230→    fetch('/api/teams').then((r) => r.json()).then(setTeams).catch(console.error)
   231→  }, [])
   232→
   233→  useEffect(() => {
   234→    if (!logScrollRef.current) return
   235→    logScrollRef.current.scrollTop = logScrollRef.current.scrollHeight
   236→  }, [logs])
   237→
   238→  function appendLog(agentId: string, missionId: string, text: string, kind: LogLine['kind']) {
   239→    setLogs((p) => [...p.slice(-500), { id: logId++, agentId, missionId, text, kind }])
   240→  }
   241→
   242→  useWebSocket((msg) => {
   243→    const { event, data } = msg
   244→    const d = data as Record<string, string>
   245→
   246→    if (event === 'agent:start') {
   247→      setAgents((p) => p.map((a) => (a.id === d.agentId ? { ...a, status: 'running' } : a)))
   248→      appendLog(d.agentId, d.missionId, `> started in ${d.workspace ?? 'cwd'}`, 'system')
   249→    }
   250→
   251→    if (event === 'agent:output') appendLog(d.agentId, d.missionId, d.text, 'output')
   252→    if (event === 'agent:error') appendLog(d.agentId, d.missionId, d.text, 'error')
   253→
   254→    if (event === 'agent:done') {
   255→      setAgents((p) => p.map((a) => (a.id === d.agentId ? { ...a, status: 'idle' } : a)))
   256→      setMissions((p) =>
   257→        p.map((m) => {
   258→          if (m.id !== d.missionId) return m
   259→          const updated = { ...m, current_stage: 'done', status: 'done' }
   260→          if (d.sessionId) updated.sessions = { ...m.sessions, [d.agentId]: d.sessionId }
   261→          return updated
   262→        })
   263→      )
   264→      appendLog(d.agentId, d.missionId, `< done (exit ${d.exitCode})`, 'system')
   265→    }
   266→
   267→    if (event === 'agent:killed') {
   268→      setAgents((p) => p.map((a) => (a.id === d.agentId ? { ...a, status: 'idle' } : a)))
   269→      appendLog(d.agentId, '', 'x killed', 'error')
   270→    }
   271→
   272→    if (event === 'message:new') {
   273→      const m = data as unknown as Message
   274→      setMessages((p) => [...p, m])
   275→      if (m.type === 'escalate') {
   276→        appendLog(m.from, m.missionId, `! escalated to ${m.to}: ${m.question}`, 'system')
   277→      } else if (m.type === 'report') {
   278→        appendLog(m.from, m.missionId, `+ report to ${m.to}: ${m.summary}`, 'system')
   279→      }
   280→    }
   281→
   282→    if (event === 'mission:done') {
   283→      setMissions((p) => p.map((m) => (m.id === d.missionId ? { ...m, current_stage: 'done', status: 'done' } : m)))
   284→    }
   285→
   286→    if (event === 'mission:updated') {
   287→      const updated = data as unknown as Mission
   288→      setMissions((p) => p.map((m) => (m.id === updated.id ? updated : m)))
   289→    }
   290→
   291→    if (event === 'task:created') {
   292→      const task = data as unknown as Task
   293→      setTasks((p) => [...p, task])
   294→    }
   295→
   296→    if (event === 'task:updated') {
   297→      const updated = data as unknown as Task
   298→      setTasks((p) => p.map((t) => (t.id === updated.id ? updated : t)))
   299→    }
   300→
   301→    if (event === 'task:deleted') {
   302→      const deleted = data as unknown as { id: string }
   303→      setTasks((p) => p.filter((t) => t.id !== deleted.id))
   304→    }
   305→
   306→    // Smart Hire events
   307→    if (event === 'hire:start') {
   308→      setHiringInProgress(true)
   309→      appendLog('hire', '', `[hire] Generating 3 candidates for: "${d.description}"`, 'system')
   310→    }
   311→    if (event === 'hire:candidates') {
   312→      const cands = (data as { candidates: Array<{ id: string; title: string }> }).candidates
   313→      appendLog('hire', '', `[hire] Generated: ${cands.map((c) => c.id).join(', ')}`, 'system')
   314→    }
   315→    if (event === 'hire:interview_start') {
   316→      appendLog('hire', '', `[hire] Interviewing ${d.candidateId}...`, 'system')
   317→    }
   318→    if (event === 'hire:interview_done') {
   319→      appendLog('hire', '', `[hire] ${d.candidateId} finished (exit ${d.exitCode})`, 'system')
   320→    }
   321→    if (event === 'hire:scores') {
   322→      const scores = (data as { scores: Array<{ candidateId: string; weighted: number }> }).scores
   323→      const summary = scores.map((s) => `${s.candidateId}: ${s.weighted}`).join(' / ')
   324→      appendLog('hire', '', `[hire] Scores: ${summary}`, 'system')
   325→    }
   326→    if (event === 'hire:complete') {
   327→      setHiringInProgress(false)
   328→      const agent = (data as { agent: Agent }).agent
   329→      appendLog('hire', '', `[hire] Hired: ${agent.title} (${agent.id})`, 'system')
   330→      setAgents((p) => [...p, { ...agent, status: 'idle' as const }])
   331→    }
   332→    if (event === 'hire:error') {
   333→      setHiringInProgress(false)
   334→      appendLog('hire', '', `[hire] Failed: ${d.message}`, 'error')
   335→    }
   336→
   337→    // Team events
   338→    if (event === 'team:created') {
   339→      const team = data as unknown as Team
   340→      setTeams((p) => [...p, team])
   341→    }
   342→    if (event === 'team:updated') {
   343→      const updated = data as unknown as Team
   344→      setTeams((p) => p.map((t) => (t.id === updated.id ? updated : t)))
   345→    }
   346→    if (event === 'team:deleted') {
   347→      setTeams((p) => p.filter((t) => t.id !== d.id))
   348→    }
   349→  })
   350→
   351→  async function createMission() {
   352→    if (!newTitle.trim()) return false
   353→    const res = await fetch('/api/missions', {
   354→      method: 'POST',
   355→      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   356→      body: JSON.stringify({ title: newTitle, type: 'engineering', workspace: null }),
   357→    })
   358→
   359→    const mission = await res.json()
   360→    setMissions((p) => [...p, mission])
   361→    setNewTitle('')
   362→    return true
   363→  }
   364→
   365→  async function startRun() {
   366→    if (!runTarget || !selectedAgent) return
   367→    const sessionId = resumeSession ? runTarget.sessions[selectedAgent] : undefined
   368→
   369→    await fetch('/api/run', {
   370→      method: 'POST',
   371→      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   372→      body: JSON.stringify({
   373→        agentId: selectedAgent,
   374→        missionId: runTarget.missionId,
   375→        prompt: runTarget.prompt,
   376→        workspace: runTarget.workspace || undefined,
   377→        resume: resumeSession && !sessionId,
   378→        sessionId,
   379→      }),
   380→    })
   381→
   382→    setMissions((p) =>
   383→      p.map((m) =>
   384→        m.id === runTarget.missionId ? { ...m, current_stage: 'development', assignee: selectedAgent } : m
   385→      )
   386→    )
   387→
   388→    setRunTarget(null)
   389→    setSelectedAgent('')
   390→    setResumeSession(false)
   391→  }
   392→
   393→  async function startSmartHire() {
   394→    if (!smartHireDesc.trim()) return
   395→    await fetch('/api/hire/smart', {

   398→      body: JSON.stringify({ description: smartHireDesc }),
   399→    })
   400→    setShowSmartHire(false)
   401→    setSmartHireDesc('')

   404→  async function fireAgent(id: string) {

   425→
   426→  async function dropOnStage(stage: string) {
   427→    if (!dragMission) return
   428→
   429→    const dragged = missions.find((m) => m.id === dragMission)
   430→    if (!dragged || dragged.current_stage === stage) {
   431→      setDragMission(null)
   432→      return
   433→    }
   434→
   435→    await fetch(`/api/missions/${dragMission}`, {
   436→      method: 'PATCH',
   437→      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   438→      body: JSON.stringify({ current_stage: stage }),
   439→    })
   440→
   441→    setMissions((p) => p.map((m) => (m.id === dragMission ? { ...m, current_stage: stage } : m)))
   442→    setDragMission(null)
   443→  }

   479→  async function pushBackTask() {
   480→    if (!pushBackTarget || !pushBackText.trim()) return
   481→    await fetch(`/api/tasks/${pushBackTarget.id}`, {
   482→      method: 'PATCH',
   483→      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   484→      body: JSON.stringify({ push_back: pushBackText }),
   485→    })
   486→    setTasks((p) =>
   487→      p.map((t) =>
   488→        t.id === pushBackTarget.id
   489→          ? { ...t, status: 'in_progress', feedback: [...t.feedback, { text: pushBackText, at: new Date().toISOString() }] }
   490→          : t
   491→      )
   492→    )
   493→    setPushBackTarget(null)

   497→  const missionsByStage = STAGES.reduce((acc, stage) => {

   499→    return acc
