  1098→                                </div>
  1099→
  1100→                                {isExpanded && replies.length > 0 && (
  1101→                                  <div className="ml-6 mt-1 space-y-1.5 border-l-2 border-[#d8e6dc] pl-3">
  1102→                                    {replies.map((reply) => (

  1104→                                        <div className="flex items-center gap-2 text-xs">
  1105→                                          <span className="font-bold text-[#26372d]">{reply.from}</span>
  1106→                                          <span className="text-[#8a9e91]">{formatMessageTime(reply.ts)}</span>
  1107→                                        </div>
  1108→                                        <p className="mt-0.5 text-sm leading-relaxed text-[#2b3a31] whitespace-pre-wrap">{reply.text}</p>
  1109→                                      </div>
  1110→                                    ))}
  1111→                                  </div>
  1112→                                )}
  1113→                              </div>
  1114→                            )
  1115→                          })}
  1116→                      </div>
  1117→                    </>
  1118→                  )}
  1119→                </div>
  1120→              </div>
  1121→            )}

  1123→
  1124→          <aside className="flex min-h-[220px] flex-col border-t border-[var(--line-soft)] bg-white/60 xl:w-[260px] xl:shrink-0 xl:border-l xl:border-t-0">
  1125→            <div className="flex items-center justify-between border-b border-[var(--line-soft)] px-4 py-3">
  1126→              <div className="section-label">Live Log</div>

  1146→
  1147→            </div>
  1148→          </aside>
  1149→        </div>
  1150→      </div>
  1151→
  1152→      {runTarget && (
  1153→        <div className="modal-mask fixed inset-0 z-50 flex items-center justify-center p-4">
  1154→          <div className="panel modal-card w-full max-w-xl p-5 lg:p-6">

  1157→
  1158→            <div className="mt-4 space-y-3">
  1159→              <label className="block text-xs font-semibold text-[#668074]">
  1160→                Workspace
  1161→                <input

  1163→                  onChange={(e) => setRunTarget({ ...runTarget, workspace: e.target.value })}
  1164→                  placeholder="/path/to/project"
  1165→                  className="input-base mt-1 font-mono text-sm"
  1166→                />
  1167→              </label>
  1168→
  1169→              <label className="block text-xs font-semibold text-[#668074]">
  1170→                Agent
  1171→                <select
  1172→                  value={selectedAgent}
  1173→                  onChange={(e) => {
  1174→                    setSelectedAgent(e.target.value)

  1183→                      {a.status === 'running' ? ' (busy)' : ''}
  1184→                    </option>
  1185→                  ))}
  1186→                </select>
  1187→              </label>
  1188→
  1189→              {selectedAgent && (
  1190→                <label className="flex cursor-pointer items-center gap-2 rounded-lg border border-[#cce7d5] bg-[#f1fff4] p-2 text-xs text-[#466858]">
  1191→                  <input
  1192→                    type="checkbox"
  1193→                    checked={resumeSession}
  1194→                    onChange={(e) => setResumeSession(e.target.checked)}
  1195→                    className="accent-[#35b562]"
  1196→                  />
  1197→                  Resume session
  1198→                  {runTarget.sessions[selectedAgent]
  1199→                    ? ` (${runTarget.sessions[selectedAgent].slice(0, 8)}...)`
  1200→                    : ' (continue latest)'}
  1201→                </label>
  1202→              )}
  1203→
  1204→              <label className="block text-xs font-semibold text-[#668074]">
  1205→                Prompt
  1206→                <textarea
  1207→                  value={runTarget.prompt}
  1208→                  onChange={(e) => setRunTarget({ ...runTarget, prompt: e.target.value })}
  1209→                  rows={4}
  1210→                  className="textarea-base mt-1 resize-none"
  1211→                />
  1212→              </label>
  1213→            </div>
  1214→
  1215→            <div className="mt-4 flex justify-end gap-2">
  1216→              <button
  1217→                onClick={() => {
  1218→                  setRunTarget(null)
  1219→                  setSelectedAgent('')
  1220→                  setResumeSession(false)
  1221→                }}
  1222→                className="btn-base btn-secondary"
  1223→              >
  1224→                Cancel
  1225→              </button>
  1226→              <button onClick={startRun} disabled={!selectedAgent} className="btn-base btn-primary">
  1227→                Run
  1228→              </button>
  1229→            </div>
  1230→          </div>
  1231→        </div>
  1232→      )}
  1233→
  1234→      {showSmartHire && (

  1348→}

data.prompt:
Design an implementation plan for ClawCorp's "auto-dispatch" feature. Here's the context:

## Current Problem
Chairman manually selects which agent to run a task on. The user (Chairman) wants: create a task → system auto-routes it through the hierarchy → executives discuss and cascade down → agents do the work.

## Current Architecture
- **Org hierarchy**: Chairman → executives (product-manager, principal-investigator) → directors (architect) → seniors/members (senior-engineer, qa-engineer, intern, research-assistant)
- **Teams**: engineering (head: architect, sponsor: product-manager), research-lab (head: principal-investigator), product (head: product-manager)
- **Existing delegate flow**: Agent calls `delegate` MCP tool → creates sub-mission → spawns sub-agent automatically
- **Agents already know how to delegate**: Their system_prompts tell them who to delegate to
- **Missions**: Created via POST /api/missions, run via POST /api/run which calls runAgent()
- **Tasks**: Simpler UI items on kanban board (backlog/todo/in_progress/review/done)

## User's Vision
"我给一个task，然后下层高管讨论，这是谁该管的，如果是技术的就是CTO的管理部分，然后就是CTO该继续往下统筹安排，最后可能安排到一个agent来做这个工作，或者几个agent合作来做这个工作"

Translation: "I give a task, then lower-level executives discuss who should handle it. If it's technical, it goes to the CTO's domain. Then the CTO continues to coordinate downward. Eventually it gets assigned to one agent or multiple agents collaborating."

## Key Design Questions

1. **Who decides routing?** Options:
   - A) LLM analyzes the task and picks the right executive (fast, simple)
   - B) Chairman's direct reports (executives) actually run as agents, "discuss" via chat, and the one who accepts it takes ownership (realistic but slow - needs all executives to be spawned)
   - C) Hybrid: LLM picks the executive, then that executive is spawned and cascades down using existing delegate tool

2. **How deep does auto-routing go?** Options:
   - A) Auto-route to executive only, executive cascades down themselves (leverages existing delegate + system_prompts)
   - B) Auto-route all the way to the leaf agent (skips middle management)

3. **Single agent or collaboration?** The user wants some tasks to involve multiple agents. This could work via the existing delegate pattern: executive delegates to multiple subordinates in parallel.

## My Recommendation
Option C + A: 
- LLM analyzes task → picks the right executive (fast routing decision)
- Spawn that executive with the task → executive uses their system_prompt + delegate tool to cascade down
- Executive naturally decides: do it themselves, delegate to one subordinate, or delegate to multiple
- This leverages ALL existing infrastructure (delegate, sub-missions, agent system_prompts)
- Chairman just sees: task created → mission auto-assigned → work cascading down in Live Log

## Files involved
- `server/src/routes/tasks.ts` — add dispatch endpoint or modify task creation
- `server/src/lib/AutoDispatch.ts` — new, routing logic (LLM call to pick executive)
- `server/src/routes/run.ts` — existing runAgent infrastructure
- `client/src/App.tsx` — replace "Run" button with "Dispatch" or auto-dispatch on task creation
- Agent profiles in `agents/` — read for routing decisions

Design a concrete implementation plan with specific file changes, function signatures, and the LLM prompt for routing. Keep it practical and minimal — build on existing patterns.

content.content:
1→import { Hono } from 'hono'
     2→import { cors } from 'hono/cors'
     3→import { logger } from 'hono/logger'
     4→import { addClient, removeClient } from './lib/hub'
     5→import { agentRoutes } from './routes/agents'
     6→import { missionRoutes } from './routes/missions'
     7→import { runRoutes } from './routes/run'
     8→import { messageRoutes } from './routes/messages'
     9→import { delegateRoutes } from './routes/delegate'
    10→import { hireRoutes } from './routes/hire'
    11→import { taskRoutes } from './routes/tasks'
    12→import { teamRoutes } from './routes/teams'
    13→import { channelRoutes } from './routes/channels'
    14→import { initChannels } from './routes/channels'
    15→
    16→const app = new Hono()
    17→
    18→app.use('*', cors({ origin: 'http://localhost:5173' }))
    19→app.use('*', logger())
    20→
    21→app.route('/api/agents', agentRoutes)
    22→app.route('/api/missions', missionRoutes)
    23→app.route('/api/run', runRoutes)
    24→app.route('/api/messages', messageRoutes)
    25→app.route('/api/delegate', delegateRoutes)
    26→app.route('/api/hire', hireRoutes)
    27→app.route('/api/tasks', taskRoutes)
    28→app.route('/api/teams', teamRoutes)
    29→app.route('/api/channels', channelRoutes)
    30→
    31→app.get('/health', (c) => c.json({ status: 'ok', time: new Date().toISOString() }))
    32→
    33→const server = Bun.serve({
    34→  port: 3001,
    35→  fetch(req, server) {
    36→    if (req.headers.get('upgrade') === 'websocket') {
    37→      const ok = server.upgrade(req)
    38→      if (ok) return undefined
    39→      return new Response('WebSocket upgrade failed', { status: 400 })
    40→    }
    41→    return app.fetch(req)
    42→  },
    43→  websocket: {
    44→    open(ws) { addClient(ws) },
    45→    close(ws) { removeClient(ws) },
    46→    message() {}
    47→  }
    48→})
    49→
    50→console.log(`ClawCorp server → http://localhost:${server.port}`)
    51→
    52→// Initialize default channels on startup
    53→initChannels().catch((err) => console.error('[channels] init failed:', err))
    54→

## My Recommendation
Option C + A: 
- LLM analyzes task → picks the right executive (fast routing decision)
- Spawn that executive with the task → executive uses their system_prompt + delegate tool to cascade down
- Executive naturally decides: do it themselves, delegate to one subordinate, or delegate to multiple
- This leverages ALL existing infrastructure (delegate, sub-missions, agent system_prompts)
- Chairman just sees: task created → mission auto-assigned → work cascading down in Live Log

## Files involved
- `server/src/routes/tasks.ts` — add dispatch endpoint or modify task creation
- `server/src/lib/AutoDispatch.ts` — new, routing logic (LLM call to pick executive)
- `server/src/routes/run.ts` — existing runAgent infrastructure
- `client/src/App.tsx` — replace "Run" button with "Dispatch" or auto-dispatch on task creation
- Agent profiles in `agents/` — read for routing decisions

Design an implementation plan for ClawCorp's "auto-dispatch" feature. Here's the context:

## Current Problem
Chairman manually selects which agent to run a task on. The user (Chairman) wants: create a task → system auto-routes it through the hierarchy → executives discuss and cascade down → agents do the work.

## Current Architecture
- **Org hierarchy**: Chairman → executives (product-manager, principal-investigator) → directors (architect) → seniors/members (senior-engineer, qa-engineer, intern, research-assistant)
- **Teams**: engineering (head: architect, sponsor: product-manager), research-lab (head: principal-investigator), product (head: product-manager)
- **Existing delegate flow**: Agent calls `delegate` MCP tool → creates sub-mission → spawns sub-agent automatically
- **Agents already know how to delegate**: Their system_prompts tell them who to delegate to
- **Missions**: Created via POST /api/missions, run via POST /api/run which calls runAgent()
- **Tasks**: Simpler UI items on kanban board (backlog/todo/in_progress/review/done)

## User's Vision
"我给一个task，然后下层高管讨论，这是谁该管的，如果是技术的就是CTO的管理部分，然后就是CTO该继续往下统筹安排，最后可能安排到一个agent来做这个工作，或者几个agent合作来做这个工作"

Translation: "I give a task, then lower-level executives discuss who should handle it. If it's technical, it goes to the CTO's domain. Then the CTO continues to coordinate downward. Eventually it gets assigned to one agent or multiple agents collaborating."

1. **Who decides routing?** Options:

   - B) Auto-route all the way to the leaf agent (skips middle management)

3. **Single agent or collaboration?** The user wants some tasks to involve multiple agents. This could work via the existing delegate pattern: executive delegates to multiple subordinates in parallel.

## My Recommendation
Option C + A:

- `server/src/routes/run.ts` — existing runAgent infrastructure
- `client/src/App.tsx` — replace "Run" button with "Dispatch" or auto-dispatch on task creation
- Agent profiles in `agents/` — read for routing decisions

content.content:
1→import Anthropic from '@anthropic-ai/sdk'
     2→import { writeFile, mkdir } from 'fs/promises'
     3→import { join } from 'path'
     4→import { existsSync } from 'fs'
     5→import { broadcast } from './hub'
     6→
     7→const AGENTS_DIR = join(import.meta.dir, '../../../agents')
     8→const ARCHIVE_DIR = join(import.meta.dir, '../../../archive')
     9→
    10→function inferTeamId(department: string): string {
    11→  const map: Record<string, string> = {
    12→    'Engineering': 'engineering',
    13→    'Research Lab': 'research-lab',
    14→    'Product': 'product',
    15→  }
    16→  return map[department] ?? 'engineering'
    17→}
    18→
    19→const TEST_TASKS: Record<string, string> = {
    20→  Engineering:
    21→    'Write a TypeScript function isPalindrome(s: string): boolean with 3 test cases',
    22→  'Research Lab':
    23→    'List 5 open research questions in the field of autonomous AI agents',
    24→  Product:
    25→    'Write 3 Given/When/Then acceptance scenarios for a user login feature',
    26→}
    27→
    28→type Candidate = {
    29→  id: string
    30→  title: string
    31→  department: string
    32→  description: string
    33→  reports_to: string
    34→  system_prompt: string
    35→  rank?: string
    36→  team?: string
    37→}
    38→
    39→type InterviewResult = {
    40→  candidate: Candidate
    41→  output: string
    42→  exitCode: number
    43→  scores?: {
    44→    task_completion: number
    45→    output_quality: number
    46→    autonomy: number
    47→    conciseness: number
    48→    weighted: number
    49→  }
    50→}
    51→
    52→export async function runSmartHire(description: string, hireId: string) {
    53→  const anthropic = new Anthropic()
    54→
    55→  try {
    56→    broadcast('hire:start', { hireId, description })
    57→
    58→    // Phase 1: Generate candidates
    59→    const candidates = await generateCandidates(anthropic, description)
    60→    broadcast('hire:candidates', {
    61→      hireId,
    62→      candidates: candidates.map((c) => ({ id: c.id, title: c.title })),
    63→    })
    64→
    65→    // Phase 2: Run interviews in parallel
    66→    const results: InterviewResult[] = []
    67→    const interviews = candidates.map(async (candidate) => {
    68→      broadcast('hire:interview_start', { hireId, candidateId: candidate.id })
    69→      const result = await runInterview(candidate)
    70→      broadcast('hire:interview_done', {
    71→        hireId,
    72→        candidateId: candidate.id,
    73→        exitCode: result.exitCode,
    74→      })
    75→      return result
    76→    })
    77→
    78→    const interviewResults = await Promise.all(interviews)
    79→    results.push(...interviewResults)
    80→
    81→    // Phase 3: Score candidates
    82→    const scored = await scoreCandidates(anthropic, description, results)
    83→    broadcast('hire:scores', {
    84→      hireId,
    85→      scores: scored.map((r) => ({
    86→        candidateId: r.candidate.id,
    87→        weighted: r.scores!.weighted,
    88→      })),
    89→    })
    90→
    91→    // Find winner (highest weighted score; tiebreak: shorter system_prompt)
    92→    const sorted = [...scored]
    93→      .filter((r) => r.scores)
    94→      .sort((a, b) => {
    95→        const diff = b.scores!.weighted - a.scores!.weighted
    96→        if (Math.abs(diff) > 0.01) return diff
    97→        return a.candidate.system_prompt.length - b.candidate.system_prompt.length
    98→      })
    99→
   100→    if (sorted.length === 0) {
   101→      broadcast('hire:error', { hireId, message: 'All candidates failed scoring' })
   102→      return
   103→    }
   104→
   105→    const winner = sorted[0]
   106→
   107→    // Phase 4: Hire winner
   108→    const profile = await hireWinner(winner.candidate)
   109→    broadcast('hire:complete', { hireId, agent: profile })
   110→
   111→    // Phase 5: Archive results
   112→    await archiveResults(hireId, description, results)
   113→  } catch (err: unknown) {
   114→    const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)
   115→    broadcast('hire:error', { hireId, message })
   116→  }
   117→}
   118→
   119→async function generateCandidates(
   120→  anthropic: Anthropic,
   121→  description: string
   122→): Promise<Candidate[]> {
   123→  const response = await anthropic.messages.create({
   124→    model: 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514',
   125→    max_tokens: 2048,
   126→    messages: [
   127→      {
   128→        role: 'user',
   129→        content: `You are a hiring manager at ClawCorp, an autonomous AI organization.
   130→
   131→The Chairman needs: "${description}"
   132→
   133→Generate 3 distinct agent profiles in JSON array format. Each should have:
   134→- id: a short kebab-case slug (e.g. "sql-optimizer")
   135→- title: a human-readable job title
   136→- department: one of "Engineering", "Research Lab", or "Product"
   137→- description: one sentence describing this agent's specialty
   138→- reports_to: infer from department — "principal-investigator" for Research Lab, "architect" for Engineering, "product-manager" for Product
   139→- rank: one of "senior", "member", or "intern" based on the complexity/seniority implied
   140→- team: the team id — "engineering", "research-lab", or "product"
   141→- system_prompt: specific, actionable instructions including the 3 mandatory rules:
   142→  1. Never ask clarifying questions — make assumptions and proceed
   143→  2. Use escalate tool if blocked
   144→  3. Use report tool when done
   145→
   146→Each candidate should have a different specialization angle or prompting strategy.
   147→Return ONLY a JSON array, no explanation or markdown fences.`,
   148→      },
   149→    ],
   150→  })
   151→
   152→  const text =
   153→    response.content[0].type === 'text' ? response.content[0].text : ''
   154→  // Extract JSON array from response (handle possible markdown fences)
   155→  const jsonMatch = text.match(/\[[\s\S]*\]/)
   156→  if (!jsonMatch) throw new Error('Failed to parse candidates from LLM response')
   157→  return JSON.parse(jsonMatch[0])
   158→}
   159→
   160→async function runInterview(candidate: Candidate): Promise<InterviewResult> {
   161→  const testTask =
   162→    TEST_TASKS[candidate.department] ?? TEST_TASKS['Engineering']
   163→
   164→  const prompt = `${candidate.system_prompt}\n\nTASK: ${testTask}`
   165→
   166→  try {
   167→    const proc = Bun.spawn(
   168→      ['claude', '--dangerously-skip-permissions', '-p', prompt],
   169→      {
   170→        cwd: '/tmp',
   171→        stdout: 'pipe',
   172→        stderr: 'pipe',
   173→        env: {
   174→          ...process.env,
   175→          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY:
   176→            process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY ??
   177→            process.env.ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN ??
   178→            '',
   179→          CLAUDECODE: undefined as unknown as string,
   180→          CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT: undefined as unknown as string,
   181→        },
   182→      }
   183→    )
   184→
   185→    // 60s timeout
   186→    const timeout = setTimeout(() => proc.kill(), 60_000)
   187→
   188→    const output = await new Response(proc.stdout).text()
   189→    const exitCode = await proc.exited
   190→    clearTimeout(timeout)
   191→
   192→    return { candidate, output, exitCode }
   193→  } catch {
   194→    return { candidate, output: '', exitCode: 1 }
   195→  }
   196→}
   197→
   198→async function scoreCandidates(
   199→  anthropic: Anthropic,
   200→  description: string,
   201→  results: InterviewResult[]
   202→): Promise<InterviewResult[]> {
   203→  const candidateSummaries = results
   204→    .map(
   205→      (r, i) =>
   206→        `## Candidate ${i + 1}: ${r.candidate.title} (${r.candidate.id})
   207→Department: ${r.candidate.department}
   208→Description: ${r.candidate.description}
   209→Exit Code: ${r.exitCode}
   210→
   211→### Test Output:
   212→${r.output.slice(0, 3000)}
   213→`
   214→    )
   215→    .join('\n---\n')
   216→
   217→  const response = await anthropic.messages.create({
   218→    model: 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514',
   219→    max_tokens: 1024,
   220→    messages: [
   221→      {
   222→        role: 'user',
   223→        content: `You are evaluating candidates for: "${description}"
   224→
   225→${candidateSummaries}
   226→
   227→Score each candidate 0-10 on these dimensions:
   228→- task_completion (40%): Did they actually do what was asked?
   229→- output_quality (30%): Is the output correct, precise, useful?
   230→- autonomy (20%): Did they make decisions without asking questions?
   231→- conciseness (10%): Did they avoid unnecessary verbosity?
   232→
   233→Return ONLY a JSON array of objects with candidateId and the 4 scores. No explanation.
   234→Example: [{"candidateId": "foo", "task_completion": 8, "output_quality": 7, "autonomy": 9, "conciseness": 8}]`,
   235→      },
   236→    ],
   237→  })
   238→
   239→  const text =
   240→    response.content[0].type === 'text' ? response.content[0].text : ''
   241→  const jsonMatch = text.match(/\[[\s\S]*\]/)
   242→  if (!jsonMatch) return results
   243→
   244→  const scores: Array<{
   245→    candidateId: string
   246→    task_completion: number
   247→    output_quality: number
   248→    autonomy: number
   249→    conciseness: number
   250→  }> = JSON.parse(jsonMatch[0])
   251→
   252→  return results.map((r) => {
   253→    const s = scores.find((sc) => sc.candidateId === r.candidate.id)
   254→    if (!s) return r
   255→    const weighted =
   256→      s.task_completion * 0.4 +
   257→      s.output_quality * 0.3 +
   258→      s.autonomy * 0.2 +
   259→      s.conciseness * 0.1
   260→    return {
   261→      ...r,
   262→      scores: { ...s, weighted: Math.round(weighted * 100) / 100 },
   263→    }
   264→  })
   265→}
   266→
   267→async function hireWinner(candidate: Candidate) {
   268→  const agentDir = join(AGENTS_DIR, candidate.id)
   269→
   270→  // Avoid collision — append timestamp if exists
   271→  const finalId = existsSync(agentDir)
   272→    ? `${candidate.id}-${Date.now()}`
   273→    : candidate.id
   274→  const finalDir = join(AGENTS_DIR, finalId)
   275→
   276→  await mkdir(finalDir, { recursive: true })
   277→
   278→  const profile = {
   279→    id: finalId,
   280→    title: candidate.title,
   281→    department: candidate.department,
   282→    description: candidate.description,
   283→    driver: {
   284→      type: 'claude-code',
   285→      command: 'claude',
   286→      args: ['--dangerously-skip-permissions', '-p', '{{full_prompt}}'],
   287→      system_prompt: candidate.system_prompt,
   288→    },
   289→    reports_to: candidate.reports_to,
   290→    subordinates: [],
   291→    rank: candidate.rank ?? 'member',
   292→    team: candidate.team ?? inferTeamId(candidate.department),
   293→    cost_model: 'medium',
   294→  }
   295→
   296→  await writeFile(join(finalDir, 'profile.json'), JSON.stringify(profile, null, 2))
   297→  await writeFile(join(finalDir, 'memory.md'), `# ${candidate.title} Memory\n\n`)
   298→
   299→  return profile
   300→}
   301→
   302→async function archiveResults(
   303→  hireId: string,
   304→  description: string,
   305→  results: InterviewResult[]
   306→) {
   307→  const dir = join(ARCHIVE_DIR, 'interviews')
   308→  await mkdir(dir, { recursive: true })
   309→
   310→  const record = {
   311→    hireId,
   312→    description,
   313→    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
   314→    candidates: results.map((r) => ({
   315→      ...r.candidate,
   316→      exitCode: r.exitCode,
   317→      output: r.output,
   318→      scores: r.scores,
   319→    })),
   320→    winner: results
   321→      .filter((r) => r.scores)
   322→      .sort((a, b) => (b.scores?.weighted ?? 0) - (a.scores?.weighted ?? 0))[0]
   323→      ?.candidate.id,
   324→  }
   325→
   326→  await writeFile(
   327→    join(dir, `H-${hireId}.json`),
   328→    JSON.stringify(record, null, 2)
   329→  )
   330→}
   331→

- **Existing delegate flow**: Agent calls `delegate` MCP tool → creates sub-mission → spawns sub-agent automatically
- **Agents already know how to delegate**: Their system_prompts tell them who to delegate to
- **Missions**: Created via POST /api/missions, run via POST /api/run which calls runAgent()
- **Tasks**: Simpler UI items on kanban board (backlog/todo/in_progress/review/done)

## User's Vision
"我给一个task，然后下层高管讨论，这是谁该管的，如果是技术的就是CTO的管理部分，然后就是CTO该继续往下统筹安排，最后可能安排到一个agent来做这个工作，或者几个agent合作来做这个工作"

1. **Who decides routing?** Options:

   - C) Hybrid: LLM picks the executive, then that executive is spawned and cascades down using existing delegate tool

2. **How deep does auto-routing go?** Options:
   - A) Auto-route to executive only, executive cascades down themselves (leverages existing delegate + system_prompts)
   - B) Auto-route all the way to the leaf agent (skips middle management)

3. **Single agent or collaboration?** The user wants some tasks to involve multiple agents. This could work via the existing delegate pattern: executive delegates to multiple subordinates in parallel.

## My Recommendation

- LLM analyzes task → picks the right executive (fast routing decision)
- Spawn that executive with the task → executive uses their system_prompt + delegate tool to cascade down
- Executive naturally decides: do it themselves, delegate to one subordinate, or delegate to multiple
- This leverages ALL existing infrastructure (delegate, sub-missions, agent system_prompts)
- Chairman just sees: task created → mission auto-assigned → work cascading down in Live Log

## Files involved
- `server/src/routes/tasks.ts` — add dispatch endpoint or modify task creation

- `server/src/routes/run.ts` — existing runAgent infrastructure
- `client/src/App.tsx` — replace "Run" button with "Dispatch" or auto-dispatch on task creation
- Agent profiles in `agents/` — read for routing decisions

1→import { Hono } from 'hono'
     2→import { readdir, readFile } from 'fs/promises'
     3→import { join } from 'path'
     4→
     5→export const agentRoutes = new Hono()
     6→
     7→const AGENTS_DIR = join(import.meta.dir, '../../../agents')
     8→
     9→agentRoutes.get('/', async (c) => {
    10→  const dirs = await readdir(AGENTS_DIR, { withFileTypes: true })
    11→  const agents = await Promise.all(
    12→    dirs
    13→      .filter(d => d.isDirectory())
    14→      .map(async d => {
    15→        const profilePath = join(AGENTS_DIR, d.name, 'profile.json')
    16→        const memPath = join(AGENTS_DIR, d.name, 'memory.md')
    17→        try {
    18→          const profile = JSON.parse(await readFile(profilePath, 'utf-8'))
    19→          const memory = await readFile(memPath, 'utf-8').catch(() => '')
    20→          return { ...profile, memory, status: 'idle' }
    21→        } catch {
    22→          return { id: d.name, title: d.name, status: 'idle' }
    23→        }
    24→      })
    25→  )
    26→  return c.json(agents)
    27→})
    28→
    29→agentRoutes.get('/:id', async (c) => {
    30→  const id = c.req.param('id')
    31→  const profilePath = join(AGENTS_DIR, id, 'profile.json')
    32→  const profile = JSON.parse(await readFile(profilePath, 'utf-8'))
    33→  return c.json(profile)
    34→})
    35→

input.content:
# Plan: Auto-Dispatch — Chairman Creates Task, System Routes Through Hierarchy

## Context

Currently Chairman must manually select which agent runs a task (click "Run" → pick agent → fill prompt). The real workflow should be: **Chairman describes work → system auto-routes to the right executive → executive cascades down through the hierarchy using `delegate` → agents do the work**. Chairman just watches progress in Live Log and Chat.

The existing infrastructure already supports the cascade: agents have `delegate` MCP tool, their system_prompts tell them who to delegate to, and sub-missions auto-spawn sub-agents. The missing piece is **automatic routing from task → executive**.

## Architecture

```
Chairman creates task: "Build a user auth system"
  │
  ▼
POST /api/tasks/:id/dispatch
  │
  ├─ LLM routing call (Sonnet, ~1s): reads executive profiles → picks product-manager
  ├─ Creates mission M-xxx, links to task
  └─ runAgent("product-manager", missionId, prompt)
       │
       ▼ product-manager spawns, reads task, decides to delegate
       ├─ delegate("architect", "Design auth architecture")
       │    └─ architect spawns → delegate("senior-engineer", "Implement JWT") → ...
       └─ report("Auth system planned and delegated")
```

One new server module (`AutoDispatch.ts`) + one new route + client UI change. Everything else reuses existing infra.

## Implementation (2 commits)

### Commit 1: Server — AutoDispatch engine + dispatch route

**File: `server/src/lib/AutoDispatch.ts`** (new)

- `autoDispatch(taskId, taskTitle, workspace?)` — main entry point
  1. Load all executive-rank agent profiles from `agents/`
  2. Load team info from `teams/`
  3. Call Claude API (Sonnet) with routing prompt → returns `{ executive_id, reasoning, suggested_approach }`
  4. Create mission (`missions/{missionId}/state.json`) linked to task
  5. Spawn executive via existing `runAgent()`
  6. Broadcast events: `dispatch:start`, `dispatch:routed`, `dispatch:spawning`, `dispatch:error`

- Routing prompt rules:
  - Software/coding/architecture → `product-manager` (oversees engineering)
  - Research/analysis/experiments → `principal-investigator`
  - Unclear → default to `product-manager`
  - Fallback if LLM fails: `product-manager`

- The prompt given to the spawned executive includes context:
  ```
  The Chairman has assigned this task to you via auto-dispatch.
  Task: {title}
  Routing reasoning: {reasoning}
  You may delegate to subordinates, cross-team delegate, or do it yourself.
  Use 'report' when done.
  ```

**File: `server/src/routes/tasks.ts`** (edit)

Add `POST /api/tasks/:id/dispatch`:
- Body: `{ workspace?: string }`
- Updates task status to `in_progress`
- Calls `autoDispatch()` (async, fire-and-forget)
- On success: links `mission_id` and `assigned_to` back to task state
- On failure: reverts task to `backlog`, sets `dispatch_error`
- Returns `{ ok: true, taskId, status: 'dispatching' }`

### Commit 2: Client — Dispatch button + Live Log events

**File: `client/src/App.tsx`** (edit)

- Extend `Task` type: add `mission_id?`, `assigned_to?`, `routing_reasoning?`
- Replace "Run" button with "Dispatch" button on task cards
  - Hidden once task has `mission_id` (already dispatched)
  - Shows assigned executive name once dispatched
- Remove `runTask()` function and the Run Mission modal (no longer needed for tasks)
- Add `dispatchTask(task)` → `POST /api/tasks/${id}/dispatch`
- Add WebSocket handlers for dispatch events in Live Log:
  - `dispatch:start` → `[dispatch] Routing: "{title}"`
  - `dispatch:routed` → `[dispatch] → {executiveId}: {reasoning}`
  - `dispatch:spawning` → `[dispatch] Spawning {executiveId}...`
  - `dispatch:error` → `[dispatch] Error: {message}`

## Files to modify

| File | Action |
|---|---|
| `server/src/lib/AutoDispatch.ts` | **Create** — LLM routing + mission creation + agent spawn |
| `server/src/routes/tasks.ts` | **Edit** — add `POST /:id/dispatch` |
| `client/src/App.tsx` | **Edit** — Dispatch button, remove manual Run, WS events |

## What already works (no changes needed)

- `delegate` MCP tool — executives cascade work down
- `cross_team_delegate` — cross-department handoff
- `runAgent()` — agent spawning with MCP injection
- Agent system_prompts — already tell agents who to delegate to
- Sub-mission creation — `delegate` route auto-creates and spawns
- Live Log streaming — all agent output already flows to UI
- Chat tab — agents' coordination visible to Chairman

## Edge cases

- **Executive busy**: `runAgent` throws → dispatch:error → task reverts to backlog
- **LLM returns bad JSON**: regex fallback + hard default to product-manager
- **LLM picks non-existent executive**: validated, falls back to first available
- **Double dispatch**: UI hides Dispatch button once `mission_id` is set

## Verification

1. Create task: "Build a REST API for user management"
2. Click "Dispatch" on the task card
3. Live Log shows: routing → picked product-manager → spawning
4. product-manager agent runs, delegates to architect
5. architect delegates to senior-engineer
6. Work cascades down, reports flow back up
7. Task card shows "product-manager" badge (dispatched)

     2→
     3→## Context
     4→
     5→The current "Hire Agent" flow is a manual form (ID, title, department, description, system_prompt). Spec 003 wants a **natural-language-in, hired-agent-out** flow: Chairman describes a need → system generates 3 candidates → each runs a standardized test in parallel → LLM scores results → best candidate auto-hired. All events stream to Live Log via WebSocket.
     6→
     7→## Architecture
     8→
     9→The heavy lifting (LLM candidate generation + LLM scoring) happens server-side. The client just provides a text input + shows progress via Live Log (already wired). We use Claude API (via `@anthropic-ai/sdk`) directly for the meta-prompts (candidate generation + scoring), NOT by spawning `claude` CLI.
    10→
    11→## Implementation (3 commits)
    12→
    13→### Commit 1: Server — Smart Hire engine + route
    14→
    15→**File: `server/src/lib/SmartHire.ts`** (new)
    16→
    17→Core engine with 3 phases:
    18→
    19→1. **Generate candidates** — Call Claude API with the spec's meta-prompt to produce 3 candidate profiles (JSON array with id, title, department, description, reports_to, system_prompt)
    20→2. **Run interviews** — For each candidate, spawn `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions -p "{test_task}"` in parallel with 60s timeout. Capture stdout as test output. Use standardized test tasks per department (from spec).
    21→3. **Score & select** — Call Claude API with a scoring prompt: pass each candidate's profile + test output, ask for 0-10 scores on 4 dimensions (task_completion 40%, output_quality 30%, autonomy 20%, conciseness 10%). Parse scores, compute weighted average, pick winner.
    22→4. **Hire winner** — Create agent directory + profile.json + memory.md (reuse existing `POST /api/hire` logic inline).
    23→5. **Archive results** — Save full interview record to `archive/interviews/H-{timestamp}.json`.
    24→

    26→
    27→**File: `server/src/routes/hire.ts`** (edit)
    28→
    29→Add `POST /api/hire/smart` route:
    30→- Body: `{ description: string }`
    31→- Validates non-empty description
    32→- Calls `runSmartHire(description)` (async, returns immediately)
    33→- Returns `{ ok: true, hireId }`
    34→
    35→### Commit 2: Client — Smart Hire UI
    36→
    37→**File: `client/src/App.tsx`** (edit)
    38→
    39→Replace the manual hire form modal with Smart Hire:
    40→
    41→- "Smart Hire" button in Agents tab header (replaces "+ Hire Agent")
    42→- Modal: single textarea for natural language description + "Start Interview" button
    43→- On submit: `POST /api/hire/smart` → close modal
    44→- Progress shown in Live Log (already handles unknown events gracefully)
    45→
    46→Add WebSocket handlers:
    47→- `hire:complete` → append new agent to agents state (same as current `hireAgent` does)
    48→- `hire:error` → show error in Live Log
    49→
    50→Keep the existing manual hire route working (just remove the UI button — API stays).
    51→
    52→### Commit 3: Client — Interview progress display
    53→
    54→**File: `client/src/App.tsx`** (edit)
    55→
    56→Add a hiring status indicator:
    57→- When `hire:start` received, set `hiringInProgress` state
    58→- Show a subtle banner or pill in the header: "Interviewing candidates..."
    59→- When `hire:complete` or `hire:error`, clear the state
    60→- Live Log handles all the detailed events automatically via existing WS handler
    61→
    62→WebSocket events to handle in Live Log (using existing `appendLog` pattern):
    63→- `hire:start` → `[hire] Generating 3 candidates for: "{description}"`
    64→- `hire:candidates` → `[hire] Generated: candidate-1, candidate-2, candidate-3`
    65→- `hire:interview_start` → `[hire] Interviewing {candidateId}...`
    66→- `hire:interview_done` → `[hire] {candidateId} finished (exit {code})`
    67→- `hire:scores` → `[hire] Scores: {c1}: {s1} / {c2}: {s2} / {c3}: {s3}`
    68→- `hire:complete` → `[hire] Hired: {title} ({id})`
    69→- `hire:error` → `[hire] Failed: {message}`
    70→
    71→## Files to modify
    72→
    73→| File | Action |
    74→|---|---|
    75→| `server/src/lib/SmartHire.ts` | **Create** — smart hire engine |
    76→| `server/src/routes/hire.ts` | **Edit** — add `POST /api/hire/smart` |
    77→| `client/src/App.tsx` | **Edit** — smart hire modal + WS events |
    78→| `server/package.json` | **Edit** — add `@anthropic-ai/sdk` dependency |
    79→| `.gitignore` | Already has `archive/` |
    80→
    81→## Key decisions
    82→
    83→- **Claude API via SDK**, not CLI spawning, for candidate generation + scoring (faster, structured output, no process overhead)
    84→- **CLI spawning for interviews** — reuse the same `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions -p` pattern that AgentRunner uses, but simplified (no MCP injection, just a raw test in /tmp)
    85→- **60s timeout** per interview candidate, all 3 run in parallel
    86→- **No "review before hire" in v1** — auto-hire the winner (P2 feature per spec)
    87→- **Archive interviews** to `archive/interviews/` for later inspection

    93→3. Type "I need a database expert who can optimize SQL queries"

# Plan: Implement Spec 003 — Smart Hire

## Context

The current "Hire Agent" flow is a manual form (ID, title, department, description, system_prompt). Spec 003 wants a **natural-language-in, hired-agent-out** flow: Chairman describes a need → system generates 3 candidates → each runs a standardized test in parallel → LLM scores results → best candidate auto-hired. All events stream to Live Log via WebSocket.

## Architecture

The heavy lifting (LLM candidate generation + LLM scoring) happens server-side. The client just provides a text input + shows progress via Live Log (already wired). We use Claude API (via `@anthropic-ai/sdk`) directly for the meta-prompts (candidate generation + scoring), NOT by spawning `claude` CLI.

## Implementation (3 commits)

### Commit 1: Server — Smart Hire engine + route

**File: `server/src/lib/SmartHire.ts`** (new)

Core engine with 3 phases:

1. **Generate candidates** — Call Claude API with the spec's meta-prompt to produce 3 candidate profiles (JSON array with id, title, department, description, reports_to, system_prompt)
2. **Run interviews** — For each candidate, spawn `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions -p "{test_task}"` in parallel with 60s timeout. Capture stdout as test output. Use standardized test tasks per department (from spec).
3. **Score & select** — Call Claude API with a scoring prompt: pass each candidate's profile + test output, ask for 0-10 scores on 4 dimensions (task_completion 40%, output_quality 30%, autonomy 20%, conciseness 10%). Parse scores, compute weighted average, pick winner.
4. **Hire winner** — Create agent directory + profile.json + memory.md (reuse existing `POST /api/hire` logic inline).
5. **Archive results** — Save full interview record to `archive/interviews/H-{timestamp}.json`.

All phases broadcast events: `hire:start`, `hire:candidates`, `hire:interview_start`, `hire:interview_done`, `hire:scores`, `hire:complete`, `hire:error`.

**File: `server/src/routes/hire.ts`** (edit)

Add `POST /api/hire/smart` route:
- Body: `{ description: string }`
- Validates non-empty description
- Calls `runSmartHire(description)` (async, returns immediately)
- Returns `{ ok: true, hireId }`

### Commit 2: Client — Smart Hire UI

**File: `client/src/App.tsx`** (edit)

Replace the manual hire form modal with Smart Hire:

- "Smart Hire" button in Agents tab header (replaces "+ Hire Agent")
- Modal: single textarea for natural language description + "Start Interview" button
- On submit: `POST /api/hire/smart` → close modal
- Progress shown in Live Log (already handles unknown events gracefully)

Add WebSocket handlers:
- `hire:complete` → append new agent to agents state (same as current `hireAgent` does)
- `hire:error` → show error in Live Log

Keep the existing manual hire route working (just remove the UI button — API stays).

### Commit 3: Client — Interview progress display

**File: `client/src/App.tsx`** (edit)

Add a hiring status indicator:
- When `hire:start` received, set `hiringInProgress` state
- Show a subtle banner or pill in the header: "Interviewing candidates..."
- When `hire:complete` or `hire:error`, clear the state
- Live Log handles all the detailed events automatically via existing WS handler

WebSocket events to handle in Live Log (using existing `appendLog` pattern):
- `hire:start` → `[hire] Generating 3 candidates for: "{description}"`
- `hire:candidates` → `[hire] Generated: candidate-1, candidate-2, candidate-3`
- `hire:interview_start` → `[hire] Interviewing {candidateId}...`
- `hire:interview_done` → `[hire] {candidateId} finished (exit {code})`
- `hire:scores` → `[hire] Scores: {c1}: {s1} / {c2}: {s2} / {c3}: {s3}`
- `hire:complete` → `[hire] Hired: {title} ({id})`
- `hire:error` → `[hire] Failed: {message}`

## Files to modify

| File | Action |
|---|---|
| `server/src/lib/SmartHire.ts` | **Create** — smart hire engine |
| `server/src/routes/hire.ts` | **Edit** — add `POST /api/hire/smart` |
| `client/src/App.tsx` | **Edit** — smart hire modal + WS events |
| `server/package.json` | **Edit** — add `@anthropic-ai/sdk` dependency |
| `.gitignore` | Already has `archive/` |

## Key decisions

- **Claude API via SDK**, not CLI spawning, for candidate generation + scoring (faster, structured output, no process overhead)
- **CLI spawning for interviews** — reuse the same `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions -p` pattern that AgentRunner uses, but simplified (no MCP injection, just a raw test in /tmp)
- **60s timeout** per interview candidate, all 3 run in parallel
- **No "review before hire" in v1** — auto-hire the winner (P2 feature per spec)
- **Archive interviews** to `archive/interviews/` for later inspection

## Verification

1. Start server + client
2. Click "Smart Hire" in Agents tab
3. Type "I need a database expert who can optimize SQL queries"
4. Click "Start Interview"
5. Watch Live Log: candidates generated → interviews running → scores → hired
6. New agent appears in org tree under correct supervisor
7. Check `archive/interviews/` for the full interview record

# Plan: Auto-Dispatch — 任务自动路由到层级链

## Context

当前 Chairman 要手动选 agent 来执行任务。真实工作流应该是：**Chairman 给一个任务 → 系统自动判断该谁管 → 派给对应高管 → 高管往下安排 → agent 做事**。Chairman 只需要观察进度。

现有基础设施已经支持级联：agents 有 `delegate` MCP 工具，system_prompt 里写了该委派给谁，sub-mission 会自动启动 sub-agent。**缺的是：任务 → 高管 的自动路由。**

## 架构

```
Chairman 创建 task: "搭建用户认证系统"
  │
  ▼
POST /api/tasks/:id/dispatch
  │
  ├─ LLM 路由 (Sonnet, ~1s): 读取高管 profiles → 选 product-manager
  ├─ 创建 mission，关联 task
  └─ runAgent("product-manager", missionId, prompt)
       │
       ▼ product-manager 启动，分析任务，决定委派
       ├─ delegate("architect", "设计认证架构")
       │    └─ architect 启动 → delegate("senior-engineer", "实现JWT") → ...
       └─ report("认证系统已规划并委派")
```

## 实现 (2 commits)

### Commit 1: Server — AutoDispatch 引擎 + dispatch 路由

**File: `server/src/lib/AutoDispatch.ts`** (new)

`autoDispatch(taskId, taskTitle, workspace?)`:
1. 读取所有 `rank=executive` 的 agent profiles
2. 读取 teams 信息
3. 调 Claude API (Sonnet, max_tokens 512) 做路由决策 → `{ executive_id, reasoning, suggested_approach }`
4. 创建 mission (`missions/{missionId}/state.json`)，关联 task
5. 调 `runAgent(executiveId, missionId, prompt)` 启动高管
6. 广播事件: `dispatch:start`, `dispatch:routed`, `dispatch:spawning`, `dispatch:error`

路由规则（写在 LLM prompt 里）:
- 软件/代码/架构/技术实现 → `product-manager`（管 engineering）
- 研究/分析/实验/调研 → `principal-investigator`
- 不确定 → 默认 `product-manager`
- LLM 解析失败 → fallback `product-manager`

给高管的 prompt:
```
The Chairman has assigned this task to you via auto-dispatch.
Task: {title}
Routing reasoning: {reasoning}
You may delegate to subordinates, cross-team delegate, or do it yourself.
Use 'report' when done.
```

**File: `server/src/routes/tasks.ts`** (edit)

加 `POST /api/tasks/:id/dispatch`:
- Body: `{ workspace?: string }`
- 更新 task status → `in_progress`
- 调 `autoDispatch()` (async fire-and-forget)
- 成功: 把 `mission_id`, `assigned_to` 写回 task state
- 失败: task 退回 `backlog`，记录 `dispatch_error`
- 返回 `{ ok: true, taskId, status: 'dispatching' }`

### Commit 2: Client — Dispatch 按钮 + Live Log 事件

**File: `client/src/App.tsx`** (edit)

- Task type 加 `mission_id?`, `assigned_to?`, `routing_reasoning?`
- "Run" 按钮改成 "Dispatch"
  - 已 dispatch 的 task（有 `mission_id`）不显示按钮，改显示负责人名字
- 删掉 `runTask()` 和手动 Run Mission modal（不再需要手选 agent）
- 加 `dispatchTask(task)` → `POST /api/tasks/${id}/dispatch`
- Live Log WebSocket 新事件:
  - `dispatch:start` → `[dispatch] Routing: "{title}"`
  - `dispatch:routed` → `[dispatch] → {executiveId}: {reasoning}`
  - `dispatch:spawning` → `[dispatch] Spawning {executiveId}...`
  - `dispatch:error` → `[dispatch] Error: {message}`

## 改动文件

| File | Action |
|---|---|
| `server/src/lib/AutoDispatch.ts` | **新建** — LLM 路由 + mission 创建 + agent 启动 |
| `server/src/routes/tasks.ts` | **编辑** — 加 `POST /:id/dispatch` |
| `client/src/App.tsx` | **编辑** — Dispatch 按钮，去掉手动 Run，WS 事件 |

## 不需要改的（已有）

- `delegate` MCP 工具 — 高管自动往下派活
- `cross_team_delegate` — 跨部门协调
- `runAgent()` — agent 启动 + MCP 注入
- Agent system_prompts — 已经写好了谁该委派给谁
- Sub-mission 创建 — delegate 路由自动创建并启动
- Live Log — agent 输出已经实时流到 UI
- Chat tab — agents 之间协调对 Chairman 可见

## 边界情况

- **高管正忙**: `runAgent` 抛错 → `dispatch:error` → task 退回 backlog
- **LLM 返回坏 JSON**: regex fallback + 硬编码默认 product-manager
- **LLM 选了不存在的高管**: 校验后 fallback 到第一个可用高管
- **重复 dispatch**: UI 隐藏 Dispatch 按钮（已有 mission_id 就不显示）

## 验证

1. 创建 task: "Build a REST API for user management"
2. 点 "Dispatch"
3. Live Log: routing → picked product-manager → spawning
4. product-manager 启动，delegate 给 architect
5. architect delegate 给 senior-engineer
6. 工作级联下去，report 逐级汇报上来
7. Task 卡片显示 "product-manager" 标签

# Plan: Implement Spec 003 — Smart Hire

## Context

The current "Hire Agent" flow is a manual form (ID, title, department, description, system_prompt). Spec 003 wants a **natural-language-in, hired-agent-out** flow: Chairman describes a need → system generates 3 candidates → each runs a standardized test in parallel → LLM scores results → best candidate auto-hired. All events stream to Live Log via WebSocket.

## Architecture

The heavy lifting (LLM candidate generation + LLM scoring) happens server-side. The client just provides a text input + shows progress via Live Log (already wired). We use Claude API (via `@anthropic-ai/sdk`) directly for the meta-prompts (candidate generation + scoring), NOT by spawning `claude` CLI.

## Implementation (3 commits)

### Commit 1: Server — Smart Hire engine + route

**File: `server/src/lib/SmartHire.ts`** (new)

Core engine with 3 phases:

1. **Generate candidates** — Call Claude API with the spec's meta-prompt to produce 3 candidate profiles (JSON array with id, title, department, description, reports_to, system_prompt)
2. **Run interviews** — For each candidate, spawn `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions -p "{test_task}"` in parallel with 60s timeout. Capture stdout as test output. Use standardized test tasks per department (from spec).
3. **Score & select** — Call Claude API with a scoring prompt: pass each candidate's profile + test output, ask for 0-10 scores on 4 dimensions (task_completion 40%, output_quality 30%, autonomy 20%, conciseness 10%). Parse scores, compute weighted average, pick winner.
4. **Hire winner** — Create agent directory + profile.json + memory.md (reuse existing `POST /api/hire` logic inline).
5. **Archive results** — Save full interview record to `archive/interviews/H-{timestamp}.json`.

All phases broadcast events: `hire:start`, `hire:candidates`, `hire:interview_start`, `hire:interview_done`, `hire:scores`, `hire:complete`, `hire:error`.

**File: `server/src/routes/hire.ts`** (edit)

Add `POST /api/hire/smart` route:
- Body: `{ description: string }`
- Validates non-empty description
- Calls `runSmartHire(description)` (async, returns immediately)
- Returns `{ ok: true, hireId }`

### Commit 2: Client — Smart Hire UI

**File: `client/src/App.tsx`** (edit)

Replace the manual hire form modal with Smart Hire:

- "Smart Hire" button in Agents tab header (replaces "+ Hire Agent")
- Modal: single textarea for natural language description + "Start Interview" button
- On submit: `POST /api/hire/smart` → close modal
- Progress shown in Live Log (already handles unknown events gracefully)

Add WebSocket handlers:
- `hire:complete` → append new agent to agents state (same as current `hireAgent` does)
- `hire:error` → show error in Live Log

Keep the existing manual hire route working (just remove the UI button — API stays).

### Commit 3: Client — Interview progress display

**File: `client/src/App.tsx`** (edit)

Add a hiring status indicator:
- When `hire:start` received, set `hiringInProgress` state
- Show a subtle banner or pill in the header: "Interviewing candidates..."
- When `hire:complete` or `hire:error`, clear the state
- Live Log handles all the detailed events automatically via existing WS handler

WebSocket events to handle in Live Log (using existing `appendLog` pattern):
- `hire:start` → `[hire] Generating 3 candidates for: "{description}"`
- `hire:candidates` → `[hire] Generated: candidate-1, candidate-2, candidate-3`
- `hire:interview_start` → `[hire] Interviewing {candidateId}...`
- `hire:interview_done` → `[hire] {candidateId} finished (exit {code})`
- `hire:scores` → `[hire] Scores: {c1}: {s1} / {c2}: {s2} / {c3}: {s3}`
- `hire:complete` → `[hire] Hired: {title} ({id})`
- `hire:error` → `[hire] Failed: {message}`

## Files to modify

| File | Action |
|---|---|
| `server/src/lib/SmartHire.ts` | **Create** — smart hire engine |
| `server/src/routes/hire.ts` | **Edit** — add `POST /api/hire/smart` |
| `client/src/App.tsx` | **Edit** — smart hire modal + WS events |
| `server/package.json` | **Edit** — add `@anthropic-ai/sdk` dependency |
| `.gitignore` | Already has `archive/` |

## Key decisions

- **Claude API via SDK**, not CLI spawning, for candidate generation + scoring (faster, structured output, no process overhead)
- **CLI spawning for interviews** — reuse the same `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions -p` pattern that AgentRunner uses, but simplified (no MCP injection, just a raw test in /tmp)
- **60s timeout** per interview candidate, all 3 run in parallel
- **No "review before hire" in v1** — auto-hire the winner (P2 feature per spec)
- **Archive interviews** to `archive/interviews/` for later inspection

## Verification

1. Start server + client
2. Click "Smart Hire" in Agents tab
3. Type "I need a database expert who can optimize SQL queries"
4. Click "Start Interview"
5. Watch Live Log: candidates generated → interviews running → scores → hired
6. New agent appears in org tree under correct supervisor
7. Check `archive/interviews/` for the full interview record

## Context

当前 Chairman 要手动选 agent 来执行任务。真实工作流应该是：**Chairman 给一个任务 → 系统自动判断该谁管 → 派给对应高管 → 高管往下安排 → agent 做事**。Chairman 只需要观察进度。

现有基础设施已经支持级联：agents 有 `delegate` MCP 工具，system_prompt 里写了该委派给谁，sub-mission 会自动启动 sub-agent。**缺的是：任务 → 高管 的自动路由。**

## 架构

```
Chairman 创建 task: "搭建用户认证系统"
  │
  ▼
POST /api/tasks/:id/dispatch
  │
  ├─ LLM 路由 (Sonnet, ~1s): 读取高管 profiles → 选 product-manager
  ├─ 创建 mission，关联 task
  └─ runAgent("product-manager", missionId, prompt)
       │
       ▼ product-manager 启动，分析任务，决定委派
       ├─ delegate("architect", "设计认证架构")
       │    └─ architect 启动 → delegate("senior-engineer", "实现JWT") → ...
       └─ report("认证系统已规划并委派")
```

## 实现 (2 commits)

### Commit 1: Server — AutoDispatch 引擎 + dispatch 路由

**File: `server/src/lib/AutoDispatch.ts`** (new)

`autoDispatch(taskId, taskTitle, workspace?)`:
1. 读取所有 `rank=executive` 的 agent profiles
2. 读取 teams 信息
3. 调 Claude API (Sonnet, max_tokens 512) 做路由决策 → `{ executive_id, reasoning, suggested_approach }`
4. 创建 mission (`missions/{missionId}/state.json`)，关联 task
5. 调 `runAgent(executiveId, missionId, prompt)` 启动高管
6. 广播事件: `dispatch:start`, `dispatch:routed`, `dispatch:spawning`, `dispatch:error`

路由规则（写在 LLM prompt 里）:
- 软件/代码/架构/技术实现 → `product-manager`（管 engineering）
- 研究/分析/实验/调研 → `principal-investigator`
- 不确定 → 默认 `product-manager`
- LLM 解析失败 → fallback `product-manager`

给高管的 prompt:
```
The Chairman has assigned this task to you via auto-dispatch.
Task: {title}
Routing reasoning: {reasoning}
You may delegate to subordinates, cross-team delegate, or do it yourself.
Use 'report' when done.
```

**File: `server/src/routes/tasks.ts`** (edit)

加 `POST /api/tasks/:id/dispatch`:
- Body: `{ workspace?: string }`
- 更新 task status → `in_progress`
- 调 `autoDispatch()` (async fire-and-forget)
- 成功: 把 `mission_id`, `assigned_to` 写回 task state
- 失败: task 退回 `backlog`，记录 `dispatch_error`
- 返回 `{ ok: true, taskId, status: 'dispatching' }`

### Commit 2: Client — Dispatch 按钮 + Live Log 事件

**File: `client/src/App.tsx`** (edit)

- Task type 加 `mission_id?`, `assigned_to?`, `routing_reasoning?`
- "Run" 按钮改成 "Dispatch"
  - 已 dispatch 的 task（有 `mission_id`）不显示按钮，改显示负责人名字
- 删掉 `runTask()` 和手动 Run Mission modal（不再需要手选 agent）
- 加 `dispatchTask(task)` → `POST /api/tasks/${id}/dispatch`
- Live Log WebSocket 新事件:
  - `dispatch:start` → `[dispatch] Routing: "{title}"`
  - `dispatch:routed` → `[dispatch] → {executiveId}: {reasoning}`
  - `dispatch:spawning` → `[dispatch] Spawning {executiveId}...`
  - `dispatch:error` → `[dispatch] Error: {message}`

## 改动文件

| File | Action |
|---|---|
| `server/src/lib/AutoDispatch.ts` | **新建** — LLM 路由 + mission 创建 + agent 启动 |
| `server/src/routes/tasks.ts` | **编辑** — 加 `POST /:id/dispatch` |
| `client/src/App.tsx` | **编辑** — Dispatch 按钮，去掉手动 Run，WS 事件 |

## 不需要改的（已有）

- `delegate` MCP 工具 — 高管自动往下派活
- `cross_team_delegate` — 跨部门协调
- `runAgent()` — agent 启动 + MCP 注入
- Agent system_prompts — 已经写好了谁该委派给谁
- Sub-mission 创建 — delegate 路由自动创建并启动
- Live Log — agent 输出已经实时流到 UI
- Chat tab — agents 之间协调对 Chairman 可见

## 边界情况

- **高管正忙**: `runAgent` 抛错 → `dispatch:error` → task 退回 backlog
- **LLM 返回坏 JSON**: regex fallback + 硬编码默认 product-manager
- **LLM 选了不存在的高管**: 校验后 fallback 到第一个可用高管
- **重复 dispatch**: UI 隐藏 Dispatch 按钮（已有 mission_id 就不显示）

## 验证

1. 创建 task: "Build a REST API for user management"
2. 点 "Dispatch"
3. Live Log: routing → picked product-manager → spawning
4. product-manager 启动，delegate 给 architect
5. architect delegate 给 senior-engineer
6. 工作级联下去，report 逐级汇报上来
7. Task 卡片显示 "product-manager" 标签
